title: Jr. Account Executive | Remote | U.S. Mortgage Company
description:
Job Type: Full-Time | Remote

Experience Level: Entry-Level (with or without experience)

Base pay of Php 19,715.00 – Php 28,600.00 + monthly performance-based incentives



We are a growing U.S.-based mortgage company looking for a Jr. Account Executive to join our team. This role is not just administrative. You will be responsible for supporting loan operations while also helping drive business growth through outbound calling, lead generation, and relationship building with broker partners.



This position is ideal for fresh graduates or early-career professionals who are confident communicators, proactive, and comfortable speaking with people, following up consistently, and building long-term professional relationships.



Key Responsibilities



Conduct outbound cold calls to prospective and existing broker partners

Perform lead generation and outreach to grow our broker network

Build and maintain strong relationships with external broker partners

Follow up consistently with brokers to drive submissions and loan volume

Assist brokers and loan processors in collecting, reviewing, and organizing borrower documents

Coordinate with brokers, processors, and internal teams throughout the loan process

Follow up on missing documents, conditions, and outstanding requirements

Upload and manage files in the loan origination system

Ensure all documents are complete, accurate, and properly labeled

Maintain file notes, status updates, and internal records

Communicate updates between underwriting, processing, sales teams, and broker partners

Help keep loan files on track to meet turnaround timelines



Qualifications



Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, or related field is a plus but not required

Strong written and verbal English communication skills

Comfortable with cold calling and outbound communication

Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment

Proactive, reliable, and results-driven

Basic knowledge of Google Workspace or Microsoft Office

Willingness to learn U.S. mortgage processes (training will be provided)



Specific Requirements



Reliable high-speed internet connection

Backup internet or data plan (mobile hotspot or secondary ISP)

Quiet and professional work environment

Willingness to work full-time during U.S. business hours (PST), equivalent to 11:30 PM to 8:30 AM Philippine time



Nice to Have



Experience in BPO, sales, lead generation, or customer service

Experience in cold calling or outbound sales

Familiarity with U.S. mortgage terms and loan process

Experience working with brokers or in relationship-based roles



Why join us?



Base pay of Php 19,715 – Php 28,600 with salary progression plus performance-based incentives

Opportunity to grow into a sales or Account Executive role

Paid training and continuous support provided

Supportive and collaborative team environment

Long-term remote role with career growth potential

Exposure to real-world U.S. mortgage operations and business development



How to Apply



Please apply through OnlineJobs.ph and follow the instructions below carefully.



Submit your updated resume in PDF format or as a link (Google Drive, Dropbox, or OLJ profile)

If submitting a link, make sure it is set to “Anyone with the link can view”

Include your relevant experience (Mortgage, Sales, BPO, Admin, or Customer Service)

Include your expected monthly salary in PHP

Include your earliest start date or availability



Please also answer the following questions in your application:



Are you aware that the working schedule is 11:30 PM to 8:30 AM Philippine time, and are you able to work consistently during these hours?

Are you comfortable making outbound calls during this schedule?

Do you have a quiet and dedicated workspace suitable for calls?

What experience do you have with cold calling or outbound sales? Please briefly describe your role and results.



Important



Start your application with this exact line: “I have read the job post from beginning to end.”



Shortlisted Candidates



Will be asked to submit a speedtest (at least 100 MBPS required; screenshot from speedtest.net)

Will provide computer specifications (16 GB RAM and processor)

Will answer short scenario-based questions



Note



Only applicants who follow the instructions above will be prioritized for review



We are looking for reliable and motivated individuals who are serious about building a long-term career in both mortgage operations and business development. If that sounds like you, apply now. We review applications daily.

title: AU Accountant
description:
AU Accountant – Virtual Assistant



Work from home · Full-time or Part-time · Open to Philippine-based applicants



About the role



We are hiring a skilled and dependable AU Accountant to work as a Virtual Assistant supporting Australian clients from the comfort of your home. This is a great opportunity for Filipino accounting professionals who want to grow their career in international accounting while enjoying a stable, remote work arrangement. You will handle bookkeeping, tax compliance, and financial reporting tasks for Australian businesses, following ATO (Australian Taxation Office) standards.



What you will do



Record and maintain daily financial transactions — accounts payable, accounts receivable, and bank reconciliations

Prepare and lodge Business Activity Statements (BAS) and Instalment Activity Statements (IAS) in compliance with ATO requirements

Process payroll, compute superannuation contributions, and submit Single Touch Payroll (STP) reports

Manage GST calculations and PAYG withholding obligations

Prepare monthly and quarterly financial reports: profit & loss statements, balance sheets, and cash flow reports

Assist in end-of-financial-year (EOFY) reconciliations and tax return preparation

Coordinate with Australian clients via
 and video calls — answering queries and providing financial updates

Maintain organised digital records using cloud-based accounting and file management tools

Provide general virtual assistant support such as data entry, scheduling, and document management

Minimum qualifications



Bachelor's degree in Accountancy, Accounting Technology, or a related field

At least 2 years of accounting experience, with exposure to Australian accounting standards preferred

Solid understanding of Australian tax laws: GST, BAS, PAYG, superannuation, and ATO compliance

Proficient in Xero, MYOB, or QuickBooks — Xero experience is a strong advantage

Excellent English communication skills — written and verbal

Strong attention to detail, organised, and able to meet deadlines independently

Reliable internet connection and a dedicated home workspace

Nice to have



CPA license (Philippine CPA board passer is welcome to apply)

Prior experience working with Australian clients or a BPO / outsourcing company

Xero certification or other cloud accounting certifications

Familiarity with AU Fair Work Act, award rates, and employment entitlements

Experience with tools like Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Slack, or Notion

What we offer



Competitive salary paid in Philippine pesos — commensurate with experience

100% work from home — no need to commute

Stable, long-term engagement with a growing client base

Supportive team environment with Australian and Filipino colleagues

Paid leave and Philippine public holiday observance

title: Billing Specialist
description:
Job Ad Description:

AAA Forklifts in Orlando, FL is seeking a detail-oriented Billing Specialist to join our growing financial team. This full-time, remote position offers competitive hourly pay depending on experience. As a valued part of our company, you'll have the opportunity to work closely with multiple departments to ensure billing accuracy and maintain strong financial operations.



Benefits:



Health coverage

PTO

Paid holidays



If you have an eye for detail, a passion for organization, and experience in QuickBooks Online or other accounting software, this could be the role you've been looking for!





THE TYPE OF BILLING SPECIALIST WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Experience with QuickBooks Online

Experience with Microsoft Office applications

General knowledge of service industry lingo

Computer literate and a fast typist

Team player with a positive attitude who can work collaboratively across departments

High attention to detail



WHAT IT'S LIKE TO BE OUR BILLING SPECIALIST

mber looks forward to coming to work and delivering exceptional results.

title: AU Bookkeeper
description:
AU Bookkeeper – Virtual Assistant



About the role



We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Bookkeeper to work as a Virtual Assistant supporting Australian small businesses and sole traders — all from the comfort of your home in the Philippines. This role is perfect for accounting or finance graduates and professionals who want to build experience with Australian clients without needing to work abroad. You will keep financial records accurate and up to date, assist with GST and BAS, and help clients stay on top of their day-to-day finances using cloud-based tools like Xero.



What you will do



Record daily financial transactions accurately in Xero, MYOB, or QuickBooks

Perform weekly and monthly bank reconciliations across multiple accounts

Process accounts payable and accounts receivable — create invoices, track payments, and follow up on overdue accounts

Categorise income and expenses in accordance with Australian chart of accounts

Assist in preparing and reviewing Business Activity Statements (BAS) for GST reporting

Process payroll and calculate superannuation contributions for Australian employees

Maintain and organise digital receipts and financial documents using cloud storage tools

Generate basic financial reports — profit & loss, aged receivables/payables — for client review

Liaise with clients via
 to clarify transactions, gather documents, and provide updates

Support the accounting team or external accountant during EOFY (end of financial year) preparation

Minimum qualifications



Bachelor's degree in Accountancy, Accounting Technology, Financial Management, or a related field

At least 1 year of bookkeeping or accounting experience (AU client experience is a plus but not required)

Working knowledge of Xero, MYOB, or QuickBooks — Xero is preferred

Basic understanding of Australian GST, BAS, and superannuation is an advantage

Good written and verbal English communication skills

High attention to detail with strong numerical and organisational skills

Stable internet connection and a quiet, dedicated home office setup

Comfortable working on AU business hours (mornings to afternoons, PH time)

Nice to have



Xero advisor or Xero payroll certification

Prior experience with an outsourcing or BPO company supporting AU/NZ clients

Familiarity with Australian payroll, award rates, and Fair Work compliance

Experience using Receipt Bank / Dext, HubDoc, or similar document capture tools

Comfortable with tools like Google Workspace, Slack, Asana, or Microsoft 365

What we offer



Competitive salary in Philippine pesos — based on your skills and experience

100% remote work — zero commute, full flexibility

Stable, long-term role with consistent hours and a supportive team

Hands-on training in Australian bookkeeping standards and tools

Paid leave and observance of Philippine public holidays

Clear growth path — opportunity to take on more responsibilities over time

title: Leasing Specialist
description:
Lead to Lease Specialist



$1000 USD / Month 



TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THIS ROLE YOU MUST SEND YOUR RESUME AND VIDEO INTRODUCTION WHEN YOU APPLY!







Job Type: Full-Time



Work Hours: Monday to Friday, an 9-hour shift including a 1-hour break (8-hour working shift, time zone to be finalized)











Job Overview: 



We are seeking a confident and driven Lead to Lease Specialist to manage inbound inquiries and proactively engage prospective residents through outbound calls. In this role, you will play a key part in converting leads into scheduled property tours by delivering a seamless and informative experience. 



The ideal candidate is highly comfortable on the phone, possesses strong communication skills, and can clearly explain the self-guided showing process while building rapport and a sense of urgency with prospects. This position requires a proactive approach, attention to detail, and the ability to effectively nurture leads throughout the leasing journey to support occupancy goals.



This role is ideal for a driven leasing professional who thrives in a fast-paced, performance-based environment and is motivated by turning leads into qualified applicants.











Key Responsibilities:



Serve as the primary point of contact for all inbound leasing inquiries (phone,
, and online leads)

Make outbound calls to new and existing leads to increase tour scheduling and application conversions

Respond promptly to new leads and follow up consistently to drive engagement

Qualify prospective residents based on rental criteria and property fit

Clearly explain the self-showing process and assist prospects with scheduling tours

Maintain accurate lead tracking and notes within company systems

Collaborate with Property Managers and Marketing to ensure alignment on availability and listing details

Follow up with prospects after tours to encourage applications









Performance Expectations:



Success in this role is measured by conversion metrics, specifically:

Leads converted to scheduled tours

Tours converted to completed applications









Required Skills & Qualifications:



Prior property management or leasing experience required

Experience with AppFolio (required)

Familiarity with Rently and Tenant Turner (preferred)

Comfortable handling both inbound and outbound calls

Strong phone presence with the ability to build rapport quickly

Clear, professional communication skills

Goal-oriented with a strong focus on conversion and results

Highly organized with strong follow-up discipline









Nice to Have:



Experience in a lead-to-lease or leasing conversion-focused role















Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

title: Senior Accountant
description:
The Role 



We’re seeking a highly motivated Senior Accountant to support our U.S. operations. This role will play a critical part in managing core accounting functions, supporting month-end close, and ensuring accuracy and efficiency across financial processes. 



The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, proactive, and experienced in handling full-cycle accounting, including overseeing Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable, while managing key financial platforms and systems. 



 



Key Responsibilities: 



Support and execute month-end and year-end close processes, including journal entries and reconciliations  

Oversee Accounts Receivable (AR) and Accounts Payable (AP) functions to ensure accuracy and timeliness  

Maintain and manage financial systems such as Intacct, credit card platforms, and reimbursement tools  

Prepare and review general ledger entries, account reconciliations, and supporting schedules  

Ensure all financial transactions are recorded in compliance with U.S. GAAP  

Assist in preparing financial reports, workpapers, and audit documentation  

Identify process improvement opportunities and support implementation of best practices  

Ensure adherence to internal controls and proper segregation of duties  

Collaborate with cross-functional teams and U.S. stakeholders to support financial operations 



Minimum Qualifications 

 



Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field  

3+ years of accounting or audit experience (Big 4 or public accounting experience is a plus)  

CPA, CIA, and/or CISA (U.S. preferred; PH credentials also considered)  

Strong knowledge of U.S. GAAP  

Experience with accounting systems such as Intacct, QuickBooks, or similar platforms  

Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Office 365  

Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple priorities  

Ability to work aligned with U.S. hours (EST/PST) 



 



Key Attributes for Success 



Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written 

Ability to prioritize, organize, manage time, and work independently to meet deadlines 

Ability to focus on small details and high-level strategic projects, 

Strong analytical, organizational, attention to detail, problem-solving, and leadership skills, 

Excellent interpersonal skills and ability to collaborate across multiple functional areas 

High degree of professionalism and tact in dealing with internal and external partners 



Perks and Benefits 



Professional Development: Structured training, mentorship, and ongoing exposure to U.S. audit tools and practices. 

Career Growth: Clear progression path from Associate to Senior roles, with performance-based evaluations. 

Global Exposure: Collaborate with U.S. engagement teams and gain international experience in public accounting. 

Work Flexibility: Remote setup with collaboration tools to stay connected and supported.

title: AU Mortgage Loan Processor
description:
AU Mortgage Loan Processor – Virtual Assistant



About the role



We are looking for a highly organised and process-driven Mortgage Loan Processor to support our Australian mortgage broking team as a Virtual Assistant based in the Philippines. In this role, you will assist mortgage brokers in processing home loan and refinancing applications from start to settlement — gathering documents, conducting serviceability checks, lodging applications through lender portals, and tracking loan progress. This is a great opportunity for finance or banking professionals who want to build a career in the thriving Australian lending industry from home.



Note: This is a loan processing and administrative support role. You will work under the supervision of a licensed Australian mortgage broker. You are not required to hold an Australian Credit Licence (ACL) or provide financial advice to clients.



What you will do



Collect, review, and organise loan application documents — payslips, bank statements, tax returns, identification, and property details

Conduct initial assessment of borrower financials including income verification, expense analysis, and basic serviceability checks

Prepare loan application packages and submit them through lender platforms (e.g., ApplyOnline, NextGen, Simpology, or lender portals)

Track loan applications through each stage — from submission to conditional approval, unconditional approval, and settlement

Liaise with lenders, valuers, conveyancers, and real estate agents to follow up on outstanding conditions and settlement requirements

Communicate with clients via
 to request missing documents and provide loan status updates

Maintain accurate and up-to-date records in CRM systems such as MyCRM, Salestrekker, Broker Engine, or similar

Prepare broker compliance documents, file notes, and ensure loan files meet Australian lending and NCCP Act requirements

Assist the broker in preparing comparison worksheets and loan scenarios for client presentations

Support post-settlement tasks including discharge requests, loan variations, and fixed-rate rollovers

Minimum qualifications



Bachelor's degree in Finance, Banking, Business Administration, Accountancy, or a related field

At least 1–2 years of experience in loan processing, banking operations, credit analysis, or a related finance role

Strong understanding of loan documentation requirements and financial statement analysis

Excellent English communication skills — confident in written correspondence with clients and lenders

Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple loan files simultaneously and meet deadlines

Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) and comfortable learning new software platforms

Reliable internet connection and a dedicated, distraction-free home workspace

Nice to have



Prior experience supporting an Australian mortgage broker or lending team

Familiarity with AU lending platforms such as ApplyOnline / NextGen, Simpology, or lender-specific portals

Knowledge of Australian lending policies, LVR (loan-to-value ratio), LMI (lenders mortgage insurance), and NCCP Act compliance

Experience with mortgage broker CRMs — MyCRM, Salestrekker, Broker Engine, or Connective Mercury

Understanding of Australian property types, title searches, and conveyancing process

Certificate IV in Finance and Mortgage Broking (AU) — not required but highly regarded

Experience using tools like Google Workspace, Slack, Trello, or Asana for remote team collaboration

What we offer



Competitive salary in Philippine pesos — based on your experience and AU lending knowledge

100% work from home — no commute, stable and long-term engagement

Full training on Australian mortgage broking workflows, lender policies, and compliance requirements

Exposure to the Australian property and lending market — a high-value, in-demand skill set

Paid leave and observance of Philippine public holidays

Supportive team environment with a direct line to an experienced Australian mortgage broker

Career growth opportunities into senior processor or broker support roles

title: medical clinic  manager
description:
Founding Virtual Practice Manager | US-Based brand new primary Medical Practice

Are you an expert in US Healthcare operations? We are Pivot Health & Lifestyle, a brand new primary care practice launching in Wilmington, NC, this May. We are looking for a Founding Virtual Practice Manager based in the Philippines to be our operational backbone.



We don't want a "virtual assistant." We want a Manager who will own the systems, lead the remote workflow, and grow with us from Day 1.



Your Mission:

Operational Architect: Set up and manage our EHR, patient scheduling, and virtual phone systems.



Revenue Cycle Support: Oversee US-based billing workflows, insurance verification, and high-level medical coding.



Patient Coordination: Manage the "Digital Front Door"—ensuring every patient gets the excellence and respectful level of service before they even walk into our Ogden clinic.



Founding Growth: Help us build the administrative SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) for our May 2026 launch.



Qualifications:

Proven US Healthcare Experience: At least 3+ years in Practice Management or high-level Medical Administration for a US-based clinic.



System Mastery: Expert knowledge of EHR systems (e.g., Athena, Elation, Kareo) and VoIP systems.



Financial Acumen: Deep understanding of US insurance, RCM, and prior authorizations.



Dependable & Team-Oriented: A proactive problem-solver who takes ownership of their work without being micromanaged.



Communication: Exceptional English verbal and written skills (Boutique-level professionalism).



Personality: Friendly, warm,  dutiful, professional, respectful and team player



Why Pivot Health?

Founding Role: You are Member #1 of our virtual team. Your voice and opinion matters.



Fixed Schedule: Standard US Eastern Time (EST) business hours (No rotating shifts).



Long-term Stability: We are building a permanent practice, not a short-term project.



Direct Partnership: Work directly with the Founder in a high-trust, respectful environment.



How to Apply:

title: IT Manager / Field Supervisor
description:
Overview

We are seeking a seasoned IT Manager / Field Supervisor to lead our Managed Service Provider (MSP) operations with a strong focus on cloud infrastructure. This role will oversee helpdesk operations, field dispatch, and project management across a diverse client base. The ideal candidate will bring deep expertise in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments, along with a proven ability to lead teams and deliver exceptional IT support in a fast-paced MSP setting.



Key Responsibilities

Lead and manage the MSP helpdesk, ensuring timely resolution of tickets and high client satisfaction

Coordinate and dispatch field technicians for on-site support and project implementations

Oversee cloud-centric IT projects including migrations, deployments, and infrastructure upgrades

Provide hands-on technical leadership in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments

Mentor and manage a team of IT professionals, fostering collaboration and growth

Monitor SLAs and KPIs, and implement process improvements for operational efficiency

Collaborate with clients to assess needs, propose cloud-first solutions, and maintain strong relationships



Required Qualifications

Minimum of 10 years in IT management, with at least 5 years in an MSP helpdesk environment leading teams

Deep technical proficiency in:

Azure AD, Office 365, Intune, SharePoint, MS Exchange Server

AWS services including EC2, S3, IAM, and VPC configurations

Windows Server (on-prem and cloud-hosted)

Google Workspace and Google Cloud infrastructure

Setup and management of Ubiquiti, Cisco, SonicWall, Synology hardware

Strong project management experience in cloud migrations and IT deployments

Proven ability to manage ticket workflows and dispatch field resources effectively

Excellent leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills

Ability to work under pressure and adapt to evolving client needs



Preferred Qualifications

Certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Cisco CCNA

Experience with MSP tools like RMM and PSA platforms

Familiarity with cybersecurity best practices and compliance standards (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR)



Why Join Us

Lead a dynamic team in a growing MSP with a cloud-first approach

Competitive salary and benefits package

Access to cutting-edge technologies and ongoing professional development

Collaborative and innovative work culture



How to Apply

Please submit your resume, a cover letter detailing your relevant experience, and a short intro video. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.



Equal Opportunity Employer

We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or any other protected status.

title: Senior Non-QM Mortgage Underwriter (Remote | Night Shift)
description:
Senior Non-QM Mortgage Underwriter (Remote | Night Shift)

Php 70,000 – Php 188,179/month (based on experience)

Full-Time | Permanent Remote | U.S. Mortgage Company



>Why This Role is Different



We’re not looking for a basic underwriter.



We’re hiring someone who can confidently review and approve complex Non-QM loans — someone who understands risk, moves fast, and knows how to make solid credit decisions.



If you have strong Non-QM experience and want to work directly with a growing U.S.-based mortgage team, this is a long-term opportunity with real impact.



> What You’ll Be Doing



Underwrite Non-QM loans (DSCR, Bank Statement, WVOE, P&L)



Analyze credit, income, assets, and collateral



Review AUS findings and apply conditions or overlays



Issue decisions: Approve, Deny, or Suspend



Work closely with processors and operations to clear conditions



Maintain strong turn times while ensuring compliance



Support junior tea
mbers when needed



> What We’re Looking For



At least 5 years U.S. mortgage underwriting experience



Strong Non-QM experience (DSCR, Bank Statement, etc.)



Experience handling self-employed borrowers and complex files



Solid understanding of U.S. mortgage guidelines (TRID, ATR/QM, etc.)



Able to make independent credit decisions



Detail-oriented and reliable in a remote setup



Plus (but not required):



Experience with Encompass



Korean, Chinese, or Vietnamese language skills



> Work Setup



Remote (Work from Home)



Night Shift: 11:30 PM – 8:30 AM PH Time



Stable, long-term role with a U.S.-based company



> Basic Requirements



At least 16GB RAM



Intel i5 (4th gen or newer) or equivalent



Stable internet (minimum 100 Mbps)



>> How to Apply



Please apply through OnlineJobs.ph and follow the instructions below carefully:



1. All applicants must submit their application via
 sent directly through OnlineJobs.ph.

Applications sent outside of OnlineJobs.ph will not be considered.



Include the following in your application message:



Updated Resume (PDF or link — Google Drive/Dropbox/OLJ profile)

If submitting a link, make sure it is set to “Anyone with the link can view”



Years of U.S. Mortgage Underwriting Experience



Years of Non-QM Experience (DSCR, Bank Statement, etc.)



Expected Monthly Salary (PHP)



Earliest Start Date / Availability



2. Answer This Question (Required)



In your application message, briefly answer:



What are the three main documents you review when underwriting a DSCR loan?



(Keep your answer short and direct.)



3. Important



To confirm you have read the job post, please include this line at the beginning of your message:



“I have Non-QM underwriting experience.”



4. For Shortlisted Candidates Only



If you are shortlisted, you will be asked to complete a quick follow-up step, which includes:



Internet speed test (screenshot from speedtest.net)



Computer specifications (RAM and processor)



Short Non-QM scenario-based questions



>> Note



Only applicants who follow the instructions above will be prioritized for review.



>> Shortlisted Candidates



Qualified applicants will be invited to complete a short screening step, which may include:



Quick technical questions (Non-QM / DSCR)



Speedtest and computer specs



Brief experience validation



>> Final Note



We’re looking for serious underwriters who know how to assess risk and make confident decisions.



If that’s you, apply now — we review applications daily.

title: Setup Specialist (Applicants must hold a Bachelor degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Accounting)
description:

Monday to Friday

RAM

 message:

title: YouTube Visual Research Assistant (Finding Ceative Visual Ideas and B-Roll))
description:
Hello!



I run a YouTube channel and need someone to help find interesting, non-obvious visuals (b-roll, clips, images) to match my scripts.  This is NOT basic research - huge emphasis on visual research!



You will:

- Read short script sections

- Suggest creative visual ideas (not always literal)

- Find real clips/images that match those ideas



Example:



If the script says:

“Two people took a substance and died”



Bad:

- Pills

- Coffins



Good:

- Empty chairs at a table

- Flickering hospital hallway lights

- Abandoned bedroom with sunlight coming through the window



That’s the level of thinking I expect.



What You’ll Do:

- Suggest 2–3 visual ideas per script line

- Find matching clips/images (YouTube, stock, etc.)

- Send links and organize everything clearly (Google Doc or similar)



Requirements:

- Strong visual taste (you can tell what feels interesting vs generic)

- Understand mood, storytelling, and tone

- NOT overly literal thinking 

- Bonus: familiarity with YouTube editing/pacing



Hours & Pay:

- ~15 hrs/week

- $400/month



To apply:



1. Start your application with the word “orange”



2. Answer this:



If the line is "He slowly lost everything," give three (3) different visual ideas that are NOT literal.



3. For EACH idea:

- Include a direct link to a real clip/image you found

- In 1–2 sentences, explain WHY you chose it and how it fits the line



Important:

Do NOT use AI tools for this. I am specifically looking for your personal thinking and taste.

title: Certified Public Accountant (CPA) - REMOTE
description:
We are a US-based custom manufacturing company, and we are looking for dedicated and loyal individuals that can contribute to our goals. We are looking for individuals who Embodies Our Core Values, Serve Others and Own It!



We are looking for Certified Public Accountants!



Key Responsibilities:

- Analyze, organize, update financial records.

- Perform regular, detailed audits to ensure accuracy in financial documents, expenditures, and investments.

- Perform daily reconciliation of credit card, bank accounts, cash accounts, spending accounts, etc.

- Setup financial accounts as needed.

- Oversee ledger reconciliation and manage accounts payable/receivables.

- Liaise with Tax Accountants to track tax payments and returns.

- Monitor bookkeeping activities regularly.

- Keep company financial information confidential.

- Ensure vendor accounts are kept up to date for the entire organization.

- Inventory Management

- China Purchase Management



Qualifications:

- Excellent FLUENT English verbal and written communication.

- Candidate must possess at least Bachelor’s/College Degree in relevant field.

- Proven work experience as a Certified Public Accountant (CPA).

- Understanding of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP).

- Preferably experienced accounting/finance employees specializing in US Accounting System.

- Familiarity with accounting applications, modules, and financial statements.

- Motivation to exceed expectations while working independently

- Friendly, fast learner who can adapt to change and have a “can-do” attitude

- Proficient computer skills in Microsoft Office, Outlook, Google, Internet Explorer, Microsoft Teams, etc.



System Requirements:

- Computer Processor: Minimum 5th generation Intel i5 processor or equivalent

- Computer Memory/RAM: Minimum 4.00 GB

- Computer Operating System: Windows 10 or Mac OSX - No Linux, Unix, or any operating system other than what is required.

- Internet Speed: Running on a 5.0 MBPS plan or higher (Both primary and backup) - If your backup connection cannot serve as your primary connection, that does not qualify as a backup connection

- REQUIRED Headset: Noise-cancelling headset connected via USB port

- REQUIRED WebCam: At least 3 megapixels capable of 720p recording



***We’re picky. You must be smart, savvy, strategic, fun, and ready to learn fast and work hard. If you’re up for the challenge, we want to hear from you!

title: VIRTUAL ASSISTANT — SHORT-TERM & MID-TERM RENTAL OPERATIONS
description:
THIS IS A THURSDAY - MONDAY FULL TIME SCHEDULE WORKING PACIFIC STANDARD TIME HOURS. 



The Link Housing LLC is a fast-growing housing company specializing in mid-term rentals for insurance placements, traveling professionals, and relocating families. We are expanding rapidly and building an operations department from the ground up.



We are hiring a high-level Virtual Assistant (VA) who is not only capable of daily operational tasks, but is also skilled in creating SOPs, building processes, and developing the operational backbone of an entire department.



This role is ideal for someone who thrives in:



An unstructured environment,



Loves building systems,



Is proactive and self-driven,



Can anticipate needs before they are stated,



And wants to grow into a leadership or Operations Manager role.



This position does not come with pre-built systems.

You will be helping BUILD this department.



PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

1. SOP Creation & Department Development



Create, write, and refine Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for every part of the business



Identify gaps in workflow and independently propose better systems



Document processes clearly and organize them into a scalable operations manual



Build repeatable workflows for:



Guest communication

Cleaning coordination

Maintenance ticketing

Onboarding new properties

Insurance communication

Work closely with leadership to shape the operational structure as the company scales

• Serve as the primary point of contact for guests: respond promptly to inquiries, manage check-ins and check-outs, and provide ongoing support

• Draft, review, and manage lease agreements for short-term and mid-term stays

• Coordinate cleaning, maintenance, and vendor services across multiple properties

• Ensure properties are consistently guest-ready through remote checklists or communication with on-the-ground teams

• Maintain and update internal records, calendars, and booking systems

• Troubleshoot and resolve guest or property-related issues professionally and efficiently

• Assist with onboarding new properties into our system and organizing operational documentation



2. Guest Communication & Hospitality



Handle guest inquiries on Airbnb and
 with warmth, professionalism, and speed

Communicate with insurance adjusters and case managers

Send booking confirmations, pre-arrival documents, weekly check-ins, and check-out messages

Follow hospitality principles to support displaced families and traveling professionals

Manage extensions, special requests, and escalations



3. Property Operations



Maintain and update 
 project boards

Coordinate cleaners (contracted vendors) and verify all turnovers with photos

Track maintenance issues and communicate with vendors when needed

Manage property profiles, inventories, utilities information, and house manuals

Assist with creating and improving listing descriptions and accuracy on Airbnb



4. Administrative Tasks



Build internal documentation for new hires and future tea
mbers

Organize data, folders, templates, and workflows inside Google Drive

Assist with onboarding new properties into our portfolio

Prepare summaries and updates for leadership

Create Leases and Run Background Checks for rentals not going through Airbnb

Reconcile Refundable Security Deposits and Aircover claims



REQUIREMENTS

Must-Have Skills:

Strong experience creating SOPs and building processes from scratch

Ability to think logically, structure workflows, and write clearly

Excellent written English and hospitality-centered communication

Experience with Airbnb messaging, property management, or guest support

Proficiency with 
 and Google Workspace

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and reliable

Self-starter who can work independently without constant instruction

Ability to make decisions and problem-solve in real time

Prior experience building an operations department

Hospitality, STR/MTR operations, or insurance housing background

Experience coordinating cleaners or contractors



HOW TO APPLY



Submit the following:

Resume

A 1-minute audio or video introduction

A photograph of your home computer set up with Airbnb on the desktop screen



Thank you and we look forward to speaking with you!

title: Lead Generation - Permanent WFH
description:
We are seeking a proactive, detail-oriented Lead Generation Specialist to drive consistent and high-quality leads from hospitals across the United States. This in-house role will be responsible for

identifying, researching, and engaging decision-makers in the healthcare industry to support our sales and marketing goals. 



The ideal candidate has experience in
 outreach, understands hospital industry dynamics, and is capable of creating and executing campaigns that align with our brand voice while building trust and opening conversations with qualified prospects. 



 



???? Key Responsibilities: 



Identify and build lists of verified hospital contacts based on our target audience (specific roles, titles, and regions). 

Conduct manual research to validate contact information and relevance. 

Develop tailored outreach messaging based on pain points and campaign objectives. 

Execute
 campaigns using platforms like SendGrid (aligned with Odoo). 

Monitor, manage, and respond to lead replies to nurture them through the pipeline. 

Maintain a consistent tone and messaging aligned with the Cube Care brand. 

Track campaign performance and report on lead quality, objections, and interest. 

Collaborate with sales and marketing to refine messaging and target segments. 

Ensure compliance with
 marketing best practices to avoid blacklisting, especially with healthcare servers. 

 



???? Key Performance Indicators (KPIs): 



Number of verified hospital contacts added monthly (target: 300+). 

Number of high-quality, engaged leads generated (target: 10–15 per month). 

 open rate (target: >35%) and reply rate (target: >8%). 

Number of
 campaigns executed per quarter (target: 2–3). 

Conversion rate from lead to opportunity (measured jointly with sales). 

Timely reporting and insights provided weekly. 

Consistency in messaging and alignment with brand tone and values. 

 



???? Requirements: 



Proven experience in lead generation, sales development, or
 outreach (healthcare industry preferred). 

Strong research skills and attention to detail. 

Familiarity with tools like SendGrid, Odoo (or similar CRM) 

Clear, persuasive written communication skills. 

Ability to work independently and manage time effectively. 

Comfortable handling large data lists and segmenting based on target criteria. 

 



???? Preferred: 



Prior experience generating leads within hospitals or the healthcare industry. 

Experience integrating CRM data for outreach and reporting. 







System Requirements:



Computer Processor: Minimum 5th generation Intel i5 processor or equivalent

Computer Memory/RAM: Minimum 4.00 GB

Computer Operating System: Windows 10 or Mac OSX - No Linux, Unix, or any operating system other than what is required.

Internet Speed: Running on a 5.0 MBPS plan or higher (Both primary and backup) - If your backup connection cannot serve as your primary connection, that does not qualify as a backup connection

REQUIRED Headset: Noise-cancelling headset connected via USB port

REQUIRED WebCam: At least 3 megapixels capable of 720p recording

title: Customer Experience Manager
description:
WFH



Position Summary



Responsibilities

Develop positive customer experiences that create client and consumer value and satisfaction, retention, and growth, building on Voice of the Customer (VOC) feedback, and qualitative and quantitative data.

Participate as a Customer Experience subject matter expert on new client/program/product launches to scope and deliver the desired customer experience for all touchpoints. This includes creating all customer experience processes and customer communications.

Support and advocate the Customer Experience on cross-functional teams (including operations, sales, creative, and technical talents) to ensure the design and delivery of a consistent and superior customer experience, when new policies and processes are drafted, and for existing policies and processes, identify and drive improvements to the customer experience.

Delivering CX projects and collaboratively working with department leads and functional heads to deliver a superior experience for our client’s consumers.

Recommend improvements based on findings and present solutions to Account Managers, and other business stakeholders for implementation and action.

Work collaboratively across departments to understand the business needs and how they contribute to the customer’s experience. Provide insights and advice to best deliver best in class customer journey.

Provide monthly NPS reports by client and survey type, and action plans to enhance overall experience.

Perform analysis on available data including VOC listening posts, operational metrics, and other variables (program type, product type, etc.) to identify relationships, trends, and opportunities for improvement.

Perform root cause analysis, including sample audits and text-data mining, to determine drivers of both dissatisfaction and satisfaction.



Requirements:

Minimum 5 years of related business experience in customer-focused management positions - where customer feedback data is continuously collected and analyzed, quality is continuously monitored, and processes are continuously improved. 

Analytical and problem-solving skills.

Working knowledge of on-line analytics as well as VOC tools and methods such as primary research and Net Promoter Score.

Skilled at translating client/customer needs to company policies, procedures, and process flow maps.

Strong oral and written communication skills with ability to interact and build relationships to effectively influence across teams and functional partners.

Strong desire and curiosity to keep abreast of customer experience industry best practices. 



Send your resume to

title: PR Manager
description:
Job description:



Position Summary



We are seeking an experienced and proactive Public Relations (PR) Manager to lead our firm's media and public relations efforts. This role plays a key part in enhancing brand reputation, increasing public visibility, and supporting our attorneys and leadership team with speaking, press, and media opportunities. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in PR strategy, event coordination, and media outreach—ideally within a legal or professional services environment.



Key Responsibilities



Media & Thought Leadership



Source and secure high-impact opportunities for podcasts, webinars, speaking engagements, and interviews



Prepare briefing documents, talking points, and bios for firm representatives

Maintain and grow relationships with podcast hosts, media contacts, and event organizers

Conference & Event Participation



Identify relevant industry conferences and speaking opportunities

Coordinate logistics for firm participation, including registration, booth materials, and scheduling

Collaborate with graphic designers to develop and print handouts, pamphlets, and branded materials

Press & Publicity



Write, coordinate, and distribute press releases for firm milestones, events, and campaigns

Maintain media lists and track PR placements

Act as a point of contact for press inquiries and media opportunities

Testimonial Collection & Promotion



Collect written and video testimonials from clients across all brands

Collaborate with the content team to ensure testimonials are edited and published appropriately

Ensure all testimonials adhere to legal marketing guidelines and ethical standards

Qualifications



Bachelor's degree in Public Relations, Communications, Marketing, or a related field

3–5 years of public relations experience, preferably in a law firm or professional services environment

Strong writing and editing skills for press materials, pitches, and media correspondence

Excellent organizational and project management abilities

Confidence in communicating with attorneys, leadership, and media professionals

Experience with PR software, press release distribution tools, and basic analytics/reporting platforms

Familiarity with ethical marketing standards, especially in the legal industry, is a plus

What We Offer



Competitive salary and performance bonuses

A collaborative, mission-driven work environment

Opportunities for professional development and career growth

Access to industry events, conferences, and networking opportunities



Job Type: Full-time

title: Assistant Property Manager (Remote)
description:
About Us

Streamline Property Management is one of the largest and fastest-growing professional property management firms in the Washington DC area. We are a third-party property manager that manages properties owned by our clients, handling everything from securing tenants and rent collection to maintenance and repairs. We pride ourselves on efficiency, excellent client service, and leveraging technology to simplify the rental process.



As a small and entrepreneurial company, we value teamwork, initiative, and the long-term growth of our people. We are building a world-class remote team and are committed to being a stable, supportive, and long-term employer for the right candidates.



The Role

We are seeking an organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Assistant Property Manager to provide dedicated remote support to our senior property manager. This is a full-time position (40 hours per week) requiring work during US Eastern Time (EST/EDT) business hours.



We are looking for someone who will act as a true right hand to our senior property manager, taking meaningful ownership of day-to-day property management tasks. You will be client-facing, tenant-facing, and vendor-facing, and must have the professionalism and communication skills to represent our company with confidence. Prior experience in property management is strongly preferred.



Responsibilities

Responsibilities will vary, but largely include the following -

•      Maintenance coordination: triage tenant requests, dispatch vendors, track work orders to completion, and follow up on invoices in AppFolio.

•      Lease & PMA drafting: prepare and manage lease agreements, property management agreements, and renewal letters using company templates.

•      Tenant & client communication: respond to inquiries, resolve issues, and keep property owners updated on leasing activity and maintenance status.

•      Leasing support: coordinate listings and showings, process applications, screen applicants, and manage move-in/move-out logistics.

•      General operations: maintain organized records in AppFolio and Google Workspace, execute SOPs, and support ad hoc projects as directed by the senior property manager.





Qualifications

•      1–2+ years of experience in property management or real estate.

•      Exceptional written and spoken English; you will communicate directly with US clients and tenants.

•      Experience with AppFolio or a comparable property management platform.

•      Proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive) and Slack.

•      Strong organizational skills and high attention to detail; accuracy in documentation is critical.

•      University or college degree in business administration or a related field.

•      Reliable high-speed internet and a quiet, dedicated home working environment.







Compensation & Benefits

•      30 hours per week, remote contractor position

•      Paid time off for vacation and holidays (20 days total per year)

•      Be part of a supportive and friendly small team

•      Opportunity to grow with a stable, expanding US-based company.

•      Stability and consistency - we are a long-term employer



How to Apply

Please submit your resume along with a short cover letter. In your cover letter, please answer the following questions:

1.    Describe your home office setup (internet speed, computer, working environment).

2.    Describe your property management experience, including any platforms you have used (e.g., AppFolio, Buildium).

3.    Share an example of a time you had to manage a difficult tenant or vendor situation — what happened and how did you handle it?

title: Fire Alarm / Low Voltage Systems Designer ($1280/ month)
description:
Location: Remote

We are seeking a skilled Fire Alarm / Security Systems Designer to join our team on a freelance, part-time, or full-time basis. The ideal candidate will have strong technical expertise in fire alarm systems, security systems, CCTV, and access control. Projects will include system design, drafting, and documentation for submittals and installations.



Responsibilities:

- Produce professional drawings and submittal packages using AutoDesk AutoCAD.

- Collaborate with project managers, contractors, and AHJs as needed.

- Revise and update designs based on feedback or code requirements.



Qualifications:

- Proven experience designing fire alarm and/or low voltage security systems.

- Proficiency in AutoCAD (required).

- Familiarity with NFPA 72 and other relevant fire/life safety codes.

- Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines.

- Good communication skills and ability to work independently.



Preferred (not required):

- Experience preparing full submittal packages for AHJ approval.

- Knowledge of manufacturer-specific equipment (e.g., Fire Lite, DMP, Kidde, Silent Knight, Honeywell, etc.).



To Apply:

- Sample projects or work portfolio (preferred).

- Availability (freelance/part
-time).

- Brief summary of relevant experience.

The ideal candidate will have strong technical expertise in fire alarm systems, security systems, CCTV, and access control. Projects will include system design, drafting, and documentation for submittals and installations.



Key Responsibilities



- Design fire alarm, security, CCTV, and access control systems in compliance with NFPA, IBC, IFC, and local codes.

- Coordinate system layouts, device locations, riser diagrams, and wiring details.

- Collaborate with project managers, contractors, and AHJs as needed.

Position Details

-Compensation: $6–$10/hour (based on experience and performance)

-Must be responsive and available to coordinate during business hours



Growth & Career Path



We’re building for the long term, and the right candidate will have the opportunity to grow with us:

-Be part of a fast-growing, tech-enabled contracting & engineering company that values operational excellence and innovation.

:

title: Inside Sales Associate | Growth Path to Appointment Setter and Closer  ($800/ Month)
description:
Inside Sales Associate | Growth Path to Appointment Setter and Closer



Job Type:

Remote |  Full-Time





About the Role



We are looking for an Inside Sales Associate to join our growing U.S.-based team, focused primarily on our Fire Alarm and Safety business.



This role is a strong fit for someone who wants to build a real future in sales. You will start by supporting lead generation, prospect research, lead organization, and follow-up coordination. As you gain experience, improve your communication, and prove your performance, you will be trained into Appointment Setting and may eventually grow into a Closer role.



This is not just a basic support position. It is a long-term opportunity for someone who is coachable, organized, strong in English, and serious about growing in sales.



Growth Path



This role is designed to develop over time:



>>Stage 1: Inside Sales Associate



>>Stage 2: Appointment Setter



>>Stage 3: Closer



We are looking for someone who is willing to learn step by step and grow with the company.



What You’ll Do

>>>Researching potential clients online

>>>Building targeted lead lists

>>>Finding key decision-makers and accurate contact details

>>>Organizing and updating lead data in spreadsheets or CRM

>>>Supporting lead qualification based on company criteria

>>>Assisting with outreach preparation and follow-up tracking

>>>Helping maintain a clean and organized sales pipeline

>>>Supporting appointment-setting efforts as training progresses



As You Grow, You May Also Be Trained On



>Lead qualification

>Outreach follow-up

>Appointment setting

>Warm lead handling

>Early-stage sales conversations

>Supporting or eventually taking on closing responsibilities



Who We’re Looking For



We want someone who is:



>>Strong in written English

>>Good in spoken English

>>Organized and detail-oriented

>>Good at online research

>>Reliable and consistent

>>Able to follow systems and instructions

>>Coachable and willing to improve

>>Interested in building a long-term career in sales



Preferred but Not Required



>>Experience in lead generation

>>Experience in inside sales

>>Experience in appointment setting

>>Experience in B2B sales

>>Background in fire alarm, low voltage, construction, engineering, or technical services

>>Experience using CRM tools or lead tracking systems



Compensation



>>Base pay to start, depending on experience

>>Commission opportunities

>>Increased earning potential as responsibilities grow

>>Performance-based advancement



********Why Join Us*******



>Clear path for growth in sales

>Hands-on training and development

>Opportunity to work in a specialized U.S. service industry

>Long-term opportunity for the right candidate

>Performance-based promotion into bigger responsibilities



*****How to Apply****



To be considered, please send all of the following:



Your resume

Applications that do not include the voice recording and 2-minute video may not be prioritized.

If you were asked to find companies that may need fire alarm and safety services, how would you identify the best leads and d
 which ones should be contacted first?

title: Bookkeeper/Financial Analyst - Multi-Entity Operations
description:
Location: Remote

Hours: Full-time, with 2-4 hours overlap with US CST





About Us



We operate a portfolio of technology businesses including an IT consulting firm serving medical/dental practices, a gaming studio, and other ventures. We're transitioning to ERPNext for our accounting operations and need a detail-oriented financial professional to own our bookkeeping and reporting functions across multiple entities.





Core Responsibilities



Daily/Weekly Operations



Reconcile bank statements across multiple business entities

Categorize and track all expenses with detailed spend analysis

Process and record transactions in ERPNext

Maintain clean separation between multiple LLCs and business units

Perform month-end close procedures



Monthly Invoicing & Collections



Generate and send all client invoices on the 1st of every month

Track invoice aging and follow up on overdue accounts

Manage accounts receivable and collection efforts

Coordinate with the operations team on billable services/hours

Maintain customer billing information and terms



Inventory Management



Maintain inventory levels in ERPNext

Monitor inventory costs and usage patterns

Coordinate with the team on reorder points

Track inventory across multiple locations/projects



Financial Reporting



Generate monthly P&L statements for each entity

Create cost center analysis (e.g., "What is the company costing us monthly?")

Develop profitability reports by business line

Prepare management reports showing cash flow and burn rates

Track project-level profitability for client engagements



Tax Preparation Support



Prepare quarterly federal tax estimate calculations

Prepare state tax estimate calculations (Nebraska primarily)

Organize documentation for CPA review and filing

Maintain tax calendar and ensure deadlines are tracked



Business Intelligence



Identify spending patterns and cost-saving opportunities

Track KPIs across business units

Answer ad-hoc questions like "How much are we spending on software subscriptions?"

Create financial dashboards in ERPNext



Required Qualifications



Must-Haves



3+ years bookkeeping experience with US companies

Experience with multi-entity accounting

Strong understanding of US GAAP

Experience with recurring monthly billing/invoicing

Proficiency in bank reconciliation and financial statement preparation

Experience preparing documentation for US quarterly taxes

Advanced Excel/spreadsheet skills

Excellent written English communication

Available for calls during US business hours as needed



Strongly Preferred



ERPNext experience (or similar ERP systems like Odoo, NetSuite, SAP)

Experience with technology/IT services companies

Inventory management experience

Cost accounting or management accounting background

Experience with project-based profitability tracking

Familiarity with US payroll systems (we use ADP)

Experience with gaming or software development accounting



Technical Requirements



Stable internet connection (must be reliable for 1st of month invoicing)

Comfortable learning new software quickly

Experience with cloud-based accounting tools

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills



What Success Looks Like



All invoices sent by noon CST on the 1st of every month (no exceptions)

Month-end books closed within 3 business days

Quarterly tax packages ready 2 weeks before deadlines

AR aging kept under 30 days average

Inventory counts are accurate and updated weekly

Management has real-time visibility into financial performance

Clean audit trail for all transactions

Proactive identification of financial insights, not just reporting



Growth Opportunities





This role has significant growth potential. As we scale from 16 to 30+ employees, this position could evolve into a Financial Operations Manager role with broader responsibilities and team management opportunities.





To Apply



Upload your resume highlighting US client experience.



Message us:

Your resume highlighting US client experience.

A brief note explaining your experience with multi-entity accounting

Your experience with monthly billing cycles and inventory management

Your monthly salary expectation in USD

Your time zone and hours of availability

Important: In your application, describe a time when you identified a significant cost-saving opportunity through financial analysis



Applications without US company experience or the cost-saving example will not be considered.





Note: We value honesty and continuous learning. If you don't know something, we expect you to say so and learn it, rather than pretend expertise you don't have. We invest heavily in training the right people.

title: A2P Support | GoHighLevel Expert
description:
I am looking for an experienced A2P (Application-to-Person) messaging specialist and GoHighLevel (GHL) expert to help resolve an ongoing issue with my SMS campaign approval process.



Job Overview



I have been trying to submit and get approval for my A2P campaign inside GoHighLevel for over a month, but my requests keep getting rejected. I need someone who understands A2P compliance, 10DLC registration, and GoHighLevel configuration to diagnose the issue, fix it, and successfully get my campaign approved so I can start sending messages to my clients.



Key Responsibilities

Review my current A2P / 10DLC registration status in GoHighLevel

Identify reasons why my campaign submissions are being rejected

Fix and optimize my business profile, brand registration, and campaign setup

Ensure full compliance with carrier requirements (T-Mobile, AT&T, Verizon, etc.)

Successfully submit and get my A2P campaign approved

Troubleshoot SMS sending issues if needed after approval

Provide clear guidance on maintaining compliance going forward

Required Skills & Experience

Strong experience with GoHighLevel (GHL) platform

Proven expertise in A2P 10DLC registration and approval process

Understanding of SMS compliance rules and carrier requirements

Experience resolving rejected campaign submissions

Ability to troubleshoot technical setup issues quickly

Goal of This Role



The main goal is simple:

Get my A2P campaign approved and fully functional so I can begin sending SMS messages to my clients without delays or compliance issues.



Preferred Candidate

Someone who has already successfully fixed A2P rejection issues or has handled multiple GHL SMS compliance setups.

If you’ve dealt with similar issues before and know how to get campaigns approved, I would like to work with you immediately.

title: Recruitment Consultant

title: Architect (Multi-Residential Experience Required) | Advanced Revit | Brisbane-Based Architecture Firm
description:

Location: Remote – Philippines

Employment Type: Full-Time (40 Hours Per Week)

Projects: Brisbane & South East Queensland, Australia



About the Opportunity



Paul Group Virtual is recruiting on behalf of a well-established and highly reputable Architecture Firm based in Brisbane, Queensland.



The firm specialises in multi-residential and mixed-use developments across South East Queensland and is seeking a Licensed Architect with strong multi-residential experience to join their team in a full-time offshore capacity.



This is a long-term position supporting Australian projects and working directly with senior architects and project directors.





Mandatory Requirements - READ CAREFULLY!!

* Licensed Architect

* Proven Australian project experience

* Minimum 5+ years professional experience

* Strong multi-residential background

* Advanced Revit proficiency (non-negotiable)

* Experience producing full construction documentation packages

* Ability to work full-time (40 hours per week)

* Experience producing multi-residential documentation sets





Project Scope

You will be involved in:

- Medium to high-density apartment developments

- Mixed-use residential buildings

- Townhouse projects

- Design development through to construction documentation



You must be confident delivering accurate, buildable documentation aligned with Australian standards.



Key Responsibilities

* Produce high-quality documentation using Revit

* Develop compliant design models

* Coordinate consultant documentation (structural, hydraulic, services)

* Ensure compliance with NCC/BCA and relevant codes

* Maintain documentation accuracy and meet project deadlines

* Participate in online coordination meetings







Technical Requirements

* 4–5+ years advanced Revit experience

* Strong construction detailing knowledge

* Understanding of Australian compliance frameworks

* Ability to work independently and manage deadlines

* Stable high-speed internet connection







Employment Details

* Long-term secure engagement

* Direct collaboration with senior architects in Brisbane

* Competitive hourly rate (AUD, based on experience)

* Paid weekly







Application Requirements

Applications will only be considered if you meet ALL of the following:



1. Short video or Loom introduction - answering these questions below:

      - Are you a Licensed Architect? Where are you registered?

      - How many years of Australian project experience do you have?

      - Describe your multi-residential experience.

      - Have you produced full construction documentation sets?

      - Confirmation of years of Revit experience



2. Submit a Multi-residential drawing samples (PDF) - HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

3. Submit yout Resume (Ensure it is accessible)

4. What is your requested hourly rate (AUD)?

5. Are you available full-time (40 hours per week)?





Submit a complete and serious application — this role can lead to long-term work and career growth with a trusted Australian firm.

title: Senior Bookkeeper Trainer/Auditor Position
description:
Company: Simpli Bookkeeping LLC

Job Type: Part-Time to Full-Time



SimpliBookkeeping is a dynamic and rapidly growing bookkeeping company seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Bookkeeping Trainer & Internal Auditor to join our team. This role plays a vital part in onboarding and developing new tea
mbers, while also conducting quality assurance audits to uphold the high standards we promise our clients.



If you’re a skilled bookkeeper who enjoys teaching others, refining processes, and ensuring accuracy across the board, we want to hear from you.



     **To Apply, Please Provide:**



- Your most up-to-date resume.



- Results of your DISC test.



-A video file where you:

   -Introduce yourself and discuss your past work experience.

   -Describe the type of work you are seeking.

   -Highlight your accounting skills and any areas for improvement.

***Only candidates with a complete profile will proceed to the interview process.***

mbers' work.

mbers grow.

At Simpli Bookkeeping, we believe in empowering our team through clarity, structure, and support. If you’re passionate about helping others grow while ensuring excellence across our bookkeeping standards, apply today — we’d love to have you join our team!
