title: Legal Document Specialist (Estate Planning & Estate Settlement)
description:
About Us

We are a growing estate planning and estate settlement law firm serving clients in Michigan and

Florida. Our firm focuses on delivering high-quality, efficient, and client-centered legal services

across all stages of estate planning and administration.

We are building a streamlined, process-driven operation and are seeking a detail-oriented Legal

Document Specialist to support document preparation and data entry for client matters.



Position Overview

This role is focused on accurate data input and document preparation, not legal advising.

You will take structured client information and input it into pre-approved legal templates to

generate documents such as:

? Wills and trusts

? Powers of attorney

? Estate settlement documents

? Initial Client Intake information



You will play a critical role in ensuring documents are complete, accurate, and properly

formatted before attorney review.



Key Responsibilities

? Input client data into legal document templates with high accuracy

? Generate first drafts of estate planning and estate settlement documents

? Review documents for completeness, formatting, and consistency

? Follow established workflows, templates, and standard operating procedures

? Flag missing or unclear information

? Organize and save documents using firm naming conventions

? Communicate with tea
mbers regarding document status and questions



What This Role Is NOT



? You will NOT provide legal advice

? You will NOT create legal language from scratch

? You will Not create final drafts of legal documents

? You will NOT communicate directly with clients unless instructed



Required Skills & Qualifications



? Strong written English skills (grammar, spelling, formatting)

? Exceptional attention to detail

? Ability to follow structured instructions and processes

? Experience with document preparation, data entry, or administrative support



Proficiency in:

? Microsoft Word / Google Docs

? File organization systems (Google Drive, Dropbox, etc. CRM)



Preferred (but not required)

? Experience working with U.S.-based law firms

? Background in estate planning, legal, or compliance work

? Familiarity with templates, forms, or document automation tools



Success in This Role

You will succeed if you:

? Catch small errors others miss

? Follow instructions exactly

? Produce consistent, clean drafts

? Work efficiently without sacrificing accuracy



How to Apply

Please submit:

1. Your resume

2. A short paragraph describing your experience with document preparation or data entry

Qualified candidates may be asked to complete a short skills test involving document formatting

and data input.



Why Join Us

? Long-term, stable role with a growing firm

? Clear processes and expectations

? Opportunity to grow into more advanced responsibilities over time

? Work directly with a professional, organized team

title: Operations Virtual Assistant
description:
Note: Interested applicants must submit their application exclusively through the OnlineJobs platform. Do not
 the company directly. Any candidate who contacts the company outside of the OnlineJobs system will be disqualified from consideration.



Operations Virtual Assistant

(Process-Driven | High-Precision | Behind-the-Scenes Operations)



Job Overview



Maid In America Cleaning Services is hiring an Operations Virtual Assistant to protect schedule integrity, data accuracy, and operational consistency.



This is a behind-the-scenes execution role. You will not be customer-facing. You will work inside defined systems and SOPs to ensure our operational engine runs cleanly and efficiently every day.



If you enjoy structured work, moving quickly without sacrificing accuracy, and taking pride in clean execution, this role will feel natural to you.



What You’ll Do:



1. Scheduling & Job Management (Primary Responsibility)

You will own scheduling preparation and system accuracy for our TN locations.



This includes:

- Creating and modifying clients, jobs, and visits in Jobber

- Preparing future-day schedules for review and approval

- Creating training tasks in Jobber for new tea
mbers

- Re-sequencing jobs and rebalancing teams within defined guardrails

- Maintaining week-to-week schedule consistency whenever possible

- Flagging and correcting conflicts or inefficiencies early

- Executing same-day scheduling adjustments exactly as directed



2. Data Accuracy & System Integrity

You will ensure operational data is accurate, complete, and reliable.



This includes:

- Updating customer notes, job frequencies, and service details

- Invoicing customers promptly after service completion

- Calculating cost-of-service data after jobs

- Preparing timesheet and tips reports for payroll

- Logging complaints and rectified cleans accurately

- Creating and publishing weekly operational reports

- Manual KPI data entry

- Verifying work-to-labor ratios using company tools



3. Administrative Support

You will support operational structure and remove administrative friction.



This includes:

- Supporting the Assistant Operations Manager with administrative execution

- Maintaining internal trackers and shared documents

- Following established documentation and checklists consistently



What This Role Requires

- High pace with high accuracy

- Comfort with structured, repeatable tasks

- Strong attention to detail

- Clear communication when issues arise

- Strong adherence defined systems and guardrails

- Ability to work independently within clear expectations



This role involves a high volume of operational tasks that must be completed correctly and consistently. As the company grows, volume will increase — and this role grows with it.



Who We’re Looking For



Detail-Oriented & Reliable

You take pride in doing things correctly and thoroughly.



High Throughput Executor

You can handle volume without losing structure or focus.



Process-Driven

You work well inside defined systems and appreciate clear expectations.



Accountable Communicator

You flag issues early, own mistakes quickly, and ask for clarification when needed.



Tech-Comfortable

You’re comfortable using tools like Slack, Google Suite, Chrome extensions, and scheduling platforms. Experience with Jobber is a strong plus.



Experienced in Fast-Paced Admin or Scheduling

Background in administrative, dispatch, logistics, scheduling, or operations-heavy roles is

preferred.



Work Requirements

- Monday–Friday, 8AM–5PM U.S. Central Time

- Reliable internet with backup

- Dedicated workspace

- Backup power options in case of outages

- Fluent written English



Our Core Values

- Communication

- Connection

- Quality

- Service

- Teamwork



If these values resonate with you, we want to hear from you.



Important Screening Step (Read Carefully)



SERIOUS APPLICANTS ONLY:

1. Complete our Culture Index survey: 

2. Submit your application with the subject line beginning with the core value you most resonate with (ALL CAPS).

3. Use a minimum of 5 Apply Points when applying

4. In your application message, include one sentence describing the most repetitive task you have performed at a high standard for 6+ months.



Applications that do not follow all instructions will not be reviewed.

title: Construction Admin & Bookkeeping Assistant
description:
About the Company

We are a fast-growing virtual firm based in British Columbia, Canada. We support a portfolio of clients across industries — including construction and property management — and pride ourselves on organized, accurate, and proactive service delivery. We're a systems-driven team that moves quickly and holds high standards.



The Role

We're looking for a detail-oriented Construction Admin & Bookkeeping Assistant to support the operational and financial administration of construction-related client files. This is a part-time remote position with room to grow for the right person.

You'll be primarily handling construction project administration — coordinating moving parts, tracking budgets, and keeping documentation tight — with bookkeeping and invoicing support layered in.



What You'll Be Doing

Subcontractor coordination and scheduling support

Managing RFIs, submittals, and change orders

Job costing and budget tracking across active projects

Progress billing, AIA-style invoicing, and invoice follow-up

Lien waivers, compliance documentation, and purchase orders

Data entry, reconciliation, and reporting in QuickBooks Online (QBO)

Professional written and verbal communication with clients and contractors — including phone calls on behalf of the team.



What We're Looking For

Required:

Proven experience in construction project administration or construction office management

Working knowledge of QuickBooks Online (QBO)

Excellent written and spoken English — you'll be communicating directly with clients and trade contractors via
 and phone

Confident and professional phone manner

Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple files simultaneously

Availability to overlap with Canadian Pacific Standard Time business hours (approximately 9am–5pm PST)



Bonus (not required, but we'll love you for it):

Familiarity with Buildertrend, Procore, or similar construction management platforms

Experience with AIA billing, lien waiver processes, or progress billing cycles

Background in bookkeeping or accounting



Who Thrives Here

You're organized, proactive, and communicate clearly without being chased. You take ownership of your files, flag issues early, and take pride in clean, accurate work. You're comfortable learning new tools and processes, and you want to be part of a team that takes its work seriously.



To Apply

Please send your application with:

A brief summary of your construction admin and/or bookkeeping experience

Software tools you've used (especially QBO, Buildertrend, Procore, or similar)

Applications submitted without requested information will not be considered.

If you’re someone who:

title: Senior Back-End Developer (Remote)
description:
DelGate Logistics is a rapidly growing Canadian 3PL company specializing in warehousing, fulfillment, and last-mile delivery

services for large and bulky products. 



Position Overview:

We're looking for a skilled Back end Engineer to help evolve and scale our back-end systems, which form the core infrastructure behind our logistics, warehouse, and delivery management platform. This role is ideal for an engineer who thrives in a fast- paced, product-driven environment and is eager to take ownership of impact back-end services used across North America. 



Key Responsibilities: 

- Design, build, and maintain backend services and APIs using Golang (primary) and Node.js (legacy support). 

- Contribute to infrastructure and deployment processes leveraging Docker Swarm and Jenkins CI/CD pipelines . 

- Collaborate with frontend and mobile engineers to ensure seamless integration and data flow across platforms. 

- Lead and support the implementation of new features, services, and third-party integrations. 

- Work with MongoDB and PostgreSQL to design efficient, reliable, and scalable data layers. 

- Support modernization initiatives while maintaining selected legacy systems. 

- Write clean, testable, and maintainable code, adhering to industry best practices and internal standards. 

- Participate in architecture reviews, agile planning, and code reviews across the engineering organization. 

- Contribute to performance monitoring, issue diagnosis, and uptime improvements. 



Qualifications: 

- 3-4 years of backend development experience, ideally within product-focused or high-scale environments. 

- Proven expertise in Golang , with hands-on experience building and maintaining production services. 

- Working knowledge of Node.js is beneficial, particularly for contributing to legacy components. 

- Experience working with MongoDB and PostgreSQL in live, production settings. 

- Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and containerized deployments (eg, Jenkins, Docker). 

- Strong understanding of RESTful API development and third-party system integration . 

- Solid grasp of backend architecture, microservices, security principles, and system scalability. 

- Comfortable working with both modern systems and legacy infrastructure. 

- Effective communicator, able to work asynchronously across global teams.



How to Apply:

Please send your updated resume and portfolio showcasing relevant experience to 



Important: The subject of your
 must be: “Senior Back-End Developer Application”

title: Creative Project Manager
description:
We're a performance-driven marketing agency working exclusively with painting contractors across the United States. We run paid social campaigns that generate real leads — and we're growing fast.



We're looking for a Creative Project Manager who sits at the intersection of strategy, creative, and execution. You'll be the connective tissue between our media buyers and video editors — translating performance data into clear creative direction, and making sure every asset gets built, delivered, and tested on time.



What you'll own

- Own the creative production pipeline end-to-end — briefing, feedback loops, deadlines, and delivery

- Act as the creative strategist between media buyers and video editors — translating ad performance data into actionable creative direction

- Build and maintain systems for creative testing, iteration, and asset management

- Write clear, detailed creative briefs that give editors everything they need without back-and-forth

- Review creative output against brief and performance benchmarks before assets go live

- Identify what's working across Meta campaigns and feed those learnings back into new creative concepts

- Keep projects moving — follow up, unblock, and hold the team to timelines



What we're looking for

- Proven project management experience in a marketing, creative, or agency environment — you've managed multiple workstreams simultaneously and never dropped the ball

- Strong understanding of Meta ads — you know what makes a good ad, what the numbers are telling you, and how to turn insights into creative action

- A creative eye — you don't need to edit video yourself, but you can spot what's working, what isn't, and why

- Exceptional written communication — your briefs are clear, your feedback is specific, and your messages don't create confusion

- Systems-oriented — you naturally build processes and aren't comfortable letting things live only in your head

- Available and responsive during EST business hours



Bonus points if you have

- Experience in home services, trades, or local service business marketing

- Hands-on experience writing or directing UGC / direct response video creative

- Familiarity with tools like ClickUp, Notion, Slack, or similar PM/ops tools



This is not the right role if

- You need to be told what to do next every day

- You've only ever been on the creative side without managing the process around it

- You're not comfortable giving direct, constructive feedback to a creative team

eting to strategize and align on PPC campaign objectives and insights.

mbers.

How to Apply:

Please send your resume and any relevant documents that showcase your PPC management skills and results via
.

.

We are eager to welcome a new member to our team and look forward to your application!

title: $1,500–$3,000 USD Base | US Executive Recruiter | Commission | Remote
description:
$1,500–$3,000 USD base + $500–$2,000 per placement. 



Build a recruiting desk with a U.S. executive search firm. Speak directly with construction executives across the United States and place leadership talent nationwide.    Top performers here grow quickly and dramatically increase their income.



This is a performance-driven recruiting role where strong producers build real income and long-term careers.



***Opportunity***

We are hiring an Executive Recruiter to help place Project Managers, Superintendents, and senior construction leaders with major contractors across the United States.



This is not a VA or sourcing-only role.



You will operate as a full recruiter, speaking with professionals, building pipelines, and helping drive real placements.

If you are competitive and motivated by performance-based income, this role offers serious earning potential and career growth.



***What You’ll Do***

• Source and engage construction professionals across the United States

• Conduct recruiting conversations with passive candidates

• Present qualified candidates to managing partners or U.S. hiring managers

• Coordinate interviews and support placements

• Build pipelines of talent for active searches



***Compensation***

Base Salary: $1,500–$3,000 USD per month

Commission: $500–$2,000 per placement

Earning Potential: Uncapped



***Who Thrives Here***

• Strong spoken English and confident phone presence

• Competitive and results-driven

• Comfortable engaging professionals who are not actively job seeking

• Organized and disciplined with follow-up



Recruiting or outbound sales experience preferred.



***About the Firm***

• Established in 1967

• Part of a network of 600+ executive search firms

• Specialized in construction leadership recruiting

• Lean, high-performance recruiting team



***How to Apply***

•Send your Resume

• Send a 1–2 minute Vocaroo or Loom voice introduction

• Your best recruiting or sales achievement

title: Animated History Shorts Creator
description:
???? AI Video Editor for YouTube Shorts (History Niche)



We are looking for a skilled AI Video Editor to help us launch and scale a YouTube Shorts

channel in the history niche.

We already operate large Instagram pages and will drive initial traffic and distribution, so

the focus is on producing high-quality, engaging short-form videos that retain attention

and go viral.



???? Role Overview



This role is strictly for video creation.

???? Scripts will be fully provided.

???? You are not responsible for writing, research, or ideation.



Your job is to take a finished script and turn it into a 40–60 second YouTube Short using AI

tools and editing software.



???? What You’ll Be Doing



- Convert provided scripts into complete short-form videos (40–60 sec)

- Generate visuals using AI tools (images, video clips, scenes)

- Add voiceover (AI or real, depending on workflow)

- Edit clips into a cohesive, engaging story

- Add subtitles and on-screen text optimized for retention

- Use pacing, cuts, and sound design to maximize watch time

- Deliver final videos ready to post



???? What We’re Looking For



- Strong editor who understands what keeps viewers watching

- Ability to translate scripts into engaging visuals

- Good sense of pacing and storytelling (even without writing the script)

- Fast and reliable execution

- Attention to detail

- Experience editing viral Shorts / Reels / TikToks



???? Output Expectations



- Initial: 1 video per day

- Ability to scale volume over time



???? Important



This is a production-focused role, NOT a creative writing role.

We already handle:

- Idea generation

- Scripting



We need someone who can execute at a high level consistently.



Only those who have attached animation examples or a portfolio including this will be considered.

title: Healthcare Sales Closer
description:
High-Ticket Closer — Dripdok (Remote, SEA)



Title is intentional: no fluff, no hype.



?



Role



You are not here to “sell.”



You are responsible for converting qualified, high-intent clients into long-term patients for a premium at-home medical and longevity service.



You will operate inside a strict, systemized discovery and closing framework.

No improvisation. No “guru tactics.” No pressure selling.



?



About Dripdok



Dripdok is a clinical-grade, at-home regenerative medicine and longevity company.



We specialize in:

 • NAD+ IV therapy

 • Peptides & advanced protocols

 • High-touch consultative care



We compete on:

 • Verification (lab-backed, traceable protocols)

 • Outcomes (not marketing claims)

 • Precision (not volume)



This is not a sales environment.

This is a high-trust, high-stakes decision environment.



?



What You Will Do

 • Run structured discovery calls (no scripts deviation)

 • Diagnose client goals, constraints, and readiness

 • Present tailored protocols and care pathways

 • Handle objections with logic, not pressure

 • Close high-ticket packages ($1k–$10k+)

 • Follow up via WhatsApp with precision and timing

 • Log all activity and outcomes in CRM



?



What You Will NOT Do

 • No cold calling

 • No fake urgency or manipulation

 • No discounting to close

 • No rewriting the offer mid-call

 • No “freestyling” your own script



Violation = immediate removal.



?



Who This Is For



You:

 • Have closed $3k–$10k offers consistently

 • Understand consultative sales (not transactional)

 • Can control a call without dominating it

 • Think in diagnosis ? prescription ? decision

 • Are comfortable discussing health, performance, longevity



You are:

 • Precise

 • Calm under pressure

 • Outcome-focused

 • Coachable within a system



?



Who This Is NOT For

 • Script readers with no thinking ability

 • “Wolf of Wall Street” closers

 • Course/crypto/agency sales backgrounds only

 • Anyone who relies on pressure tactics



Commission



On top of base pay there is commission



Compensation is performance-based, with commission ranging from 10% to 15% on cash collected.

There is additional upside for sustained performance above a 30% close rate.



There is no cap, but earnings are directly tied to your ability to execute within the system.

title: Virtual Assistant (VA)
description:
We are a fast-growing, U.S.-based luxury cleaning service company focused on building efficient, long-term systems and scalable processes. We’re seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Multifunctional Virtual Assistant who is eager to learn new tools and workflows over time.



This is a long-term opportunity ideal for someone who enjoys structure, documentation, research, and continuously improving systems.



Core Responsibilities



1. Document Organization & Administration



Organize and manage internal documents, including employee forms, agreements, SOPs, and onboarding materials

Create and maintain clear, logical folder structures in Google Drive or Dropbox

Ensure all documents are properly labeled, version-controlled, and easily accessible



2. SOP & Process Documentation



Review provided YouTube content related to professional and luxury cleaning

Extract key steps, tools, and techniques

Transform video content into clear, structured written SOPs and checklists (e.g., deep cleaning, specialty services, future white-glove offerings)

Continuously refine and improve documentation



3. Platform & Systems Support (Training Provided)



Learn and assist with BookingKoala setup and optimization

Help align services, pricing structures, and descriptions with the company website

Document platform workflows for future team reference

(No prior BookingKoala experience required — willingness to learn is key)



4. Market & Competitor Research



Research competitors in the cleaning and luxury services space

Analyze and summarize pricing models, service offerings, and market positioning

Provide clear, written insights (not just links)



5. Social Media Support



Set up and manage social media accounts (e.g., Instagram, TikTok)

Repurpose content ideas from provided materials

Draft captions, hashtags, and posting schedules

Consistently publish content following brand guidelines

Required Skills & Traits

Excellent written English

Strong attention to detail

Highly organized and process-driven

Comfortable learning new tools and systems

Ability to follow instructions accurately

Proactive communicator who asks thoughtful questions

Reliable with consistent availability

Fluent in English

title: FULLTIME Amazon Admin & Sourcing Virtual Assistant!
description:
Job Post Title: 

FULLTIME Amazon Admin & Sourcing Virtual Assistant!



About Our Company:

We are a family-owned Amazon business based in the US market, and we're looking to grow. We believe in providing a secure, long-term working environment. In this economy, we understand the value of stability, and we want you to have peace of mind knowing you have a reliable and permanent position with us.



The Opportunity:

This is a full-time position for a motivated, self-starter to be a key part of our operations. While the role starts at $500 a month, we are looking for someone who wants to grow with us. As our business expands, your responsibilities and compensation will grow along with it. The sky is the limit for our growth, and we are looking for an ambitious individual who is ready to take on more and build a career, not just have a job.



Key Responsibilities:



1.) Sourcing and Product Research:



Proactively find profitable products for our online arbitrage business using our established criteria and your own initiative.

Research trends and sales to identify new opportunities.



2.) Administrative Tasks:



Handle data entry, ensuring all product and sourcing information is accurate and organized.



Manage and track orders, ensuring timely and correct delivery of inventory.



Maintain detailed records and reports in Google Sheets.



Check for missing inventory: Run the "Reconcile" report for all FBA shipments that were recently closed.



Create a case with Seller Support for any units that Amazon received but have not been added to your inventory.



Track reimbursements: Go to your "Reimbursements" dashboard to ensure all pending cases are being processed. Follow up on any cases that are past the expected resolution time.



How to Apply:

To be considered for this position, please send your resume and a brief cover letter. Additionally, please create a 30-second to 1-minute video introduction of yourself, upload it, and include the link in your reply. Also, please use the following subject line: [INSERT Your Favorite TV Show]



3.) General Backend Support:



Provide support for various Amazon Seller Central administrative tasks as needed.



Assist with other administrative duties as required to support our business operations.



We Are Looking For Someone Who Is:



A Self-Starter: You don't wait to be told what to do. You take initiative and are resourceful.



Ambitious and Has a Growth Mindset: You want to learn and take on more responsibility, seeing this role as a stepping stone to a bigger career.



Detail-Oriented: You have an eye for detail and ensure all data is accurate.



Trustworthy: You are reliable and honest.



Strong Communicator: You can communicate clearly and effectively in English + Fast Computer



Must be able to work USA Timezone!



Compensation & Benefits:

-Salary: A stable full-time base salary of $500 per month.

— Opportunity to earn $550 USD / mo after 6 months if performance milestones are met

— Opportunity to earn $600 USD / mo after 12 months is performance milestones are met



-Security: This is a long-term position, providing you with job security you can rely on.



-Growth: Significant room to grow in both responsibility and salary as we grow the business. Career advancement (promotions)



— We also offer quarterly and annual performance-based bonuses

— We honor the tradition of 13th pay

— Stable Career and career advancement (promotions)

title: Video Editor – Short Form (Flexible Schedule)
description:
We’re Hiring Remote AI Video Clip Editors



We’re expanding our content team and looking for reliable Short-Form Video Editors to help process high volumes of short real estate content using AI-powered editing software.



We are a fast-growing U.S.-based real estate company producing daily short-form video across multiple platforms.



This is a remote, flexible, task-based position.



If you’re comfortable working with AI tools and enjoy structured, repeatable workflows, this could be a strong fit.



What You’ll Do



• Use AI editing software to process short-form video clips

• Clean up, trim, and format videos for social platforms

• Apply simple style guidelines and formatting standards (provided)

• Upload and submit completed clips by deadline



No filming

No scripting

No content strategy required

This is execution-focused editing work.



Compensation



Pay is based on finished video length:



• $0.25 USD per finished minute

• 1-minute video = $0.25

• 3-minute video = $0.75

• 4-minute video = $1.00



Most videos are 1–3 minutes long.



• Paid per completed video

• Unlimited earning potential

• Consistent volume available



The more videos you complete accurately and on time, the more you earn.



Schedule



• Fully remote

• 100% flexible schedule

• No fixed hours

• No required shifts



This is output-based work, not hourly.



Ideal Candidate



This role is a strong fit if:



• You’re comfortable using AI tools (or can learn quickly)

• You prefer structured, process-driven tasks

• You’re detail-oriented and follow instructions closely

• You want flexible, independent work

• You’re consistent and dependable

title: GOOGLE ADS MANAGER
description:
This is NOT a beginner role.

This is NOT a general VA role.

This is NOT a graphic design or video editing role.

This is NOT an SEO-only role.



This is a hands-on Google Ads management role focused on testing, optimization, and performance improvement.



We are a fast-growing Canadian property management and real estate company.

You’ll be supporting active Google Ads campaigns that generate real leads for a real business.



If you like finding performance issues, improving campaigns week over week, and making decisions based on real data, you’ll likely be a strong fit for this role.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

- Google Ads (Primary Responsibility)

- Manage and optimize live Google Ads campaigns

- Work inside Search, Display, and Performance Max

- Monitor results and identify drops or problems

- Test ad copy, targeting, structure, and messaging

- Make weekly optimizations based on real data

- Prepare simple weekly summaries (what changed + trend)



Google Business Profile (Secondary Support)

- Post weekly updates, photos, and linked content

- Reply to Google reviews

- Help keep the profile active and optimized

- Support local visibility through consistent GBP activity



ACCOUNT ACCESS (SECURITY)

You can do planning and prep work on your own computer.

All live ad accounts and related accounts are accessed through our Canadian Linux workstation via ThinLinc.



MUST-HAVES

- Proven hands-on experience managing live Google Ads campaigns

- Strong understanding of Search, Display, and/or Performance Max

- Ability to troubleshoot performance drops and improve results

- Proof of work (screenshots, results summary, or portfolio)

- Comfortable using AI tools (we use them daily)

- Reliable internet (backup connection is a plus)



THIS IS NOT A ROLE FOR

- Beginners

- General virtual assistants

- Graphic designers

- Video editors

- SEO-only applicants

- “Boost post only” media buyers

- Applicants without real Google Ads campaign experience



TOOLS YOU SHOULD KNOW

- Google Ads 

- Google Business Profile

- Google Workspace

- AI tools (ChatGPT, etc.)



PAY + HOURS

- Guaranteed base salary: $800 CAD per month

- Base covers core expected hours

- Any work outside the base is paid at $8 CAD/hour

- 30 hours per week to start

- Long-term role

- Full pay structure and growth path will be explained during the interview



HOW TO APPLY

SUBJECT LINE MUST BE:

GOOGLE ADS



Send:

- Your resume

- Proof of results or hands-on Google Ads campaign experience

- Which Google campaign types you’ve managed most (Search / Display / PMax)

- In 3 bullets: what you check first when Google Ads performance drops

- In 3–5 bullets: how you d
 whether a GBP asset is worth posting, needs improvement, or should not be used at all

- Confirm your internet speed and backup plan

- Confirm you are comfortable using ThinLinc



Only applicants who fully follow the instructions above will be reviewed and shortlisted.

title: API & Automation Specialist (Zapier / Google Sheets / Dashboards)
description:
Part-Time / Ongoing

What We Need

We run a high-volume HVAC installation company and need someone to connect our systems, automate workflows, and build clean dashboards.

This is a hands-on execution role — not theory, not “data science.”

If you like building systems that make operations run smoother, this is for you.

Your Core Work



Build API integrations (Housecall Pro ? Google Sheets ? Slack)

Create automations using Zapier / Make (webhooks, triggers, logic)

Build Google Sheets dashboards for KPIs:

Callback rates

On-time completion

Install performance



Set up Slack alerts based on job events (checklists, job status, etc.)

Clean up messy processes and make them efficient

Must Have



Proven experience with API integrations (REST / webhooks)

Strong Zapier or Make experience

Advanced Google Sheets (formulas + dashboards)

Ability to think in systems, not just tasks

Nice to Have



Google Apps Script

CRM experience (especially Housecall Pro or similar)

Operations/process improvement background

Test (Required to Apply)

Answer this briefly:

Your answer to the test above

We’re looking for someone who builds and automates real systems.

title: Onboarding & Listing Specialist
description:
We are a short-term rental co-hosting company managing roughly 56 properties across 15 states. We operate under a co-hosting model — property owners retain their listings, reviews, and branding while we handle operations, guest experience, pricing, cleaning, maintenance, and reporting. We compete on quality, not quantity, and we onboard 1–2 new properties per month with a target of scaling to 200 properties by 2028.



Onboarding is the foundation of every client relationship. A property that is onboarded correctly generates clean, stable operations for years. A property that is onboarded poorly generates ongoing friction that drains the entire operations team. We are hiring for an Onboarding & Listing Specialist who owns this function end-to-end.



Role Mission

Take every new property from signed contract to first booking in a defined, repeatable, high-quality process. You are the first operational touchpoint for every new owner and the architect of the conditions under which every other tea
mber succeeds or struggles.



Key Outcomes (What Success Looks Like in 90 Days)

• Time from signed contract to first live booking: under 21 days on 90%+ of new properties.

• Owner satisfaction score on onboarding: 9/10 or higher measured via post-onboarding survey.

• Zero listing errors at go-live — amenities, photos, pricing, house rules, and descriptions verified and correct.

• Pricing strategy configured correctly on day one — dynamic pricing tools set up with appropriate base rates, seasonality, and event-based adjustments.

• Full operational handoff document complete for every new property before go-live — cleaning, maintenance, guest experience, and pricing teams receive complete information.

• Onboarding SOP maintained and improved quarterly based on what worked and what broke.



Core Responsibilities



Owner-Facing Onboarding

• Conduct the kickoff call with every new owner — welcome, process walkthrough, expectations, timeline.

• Collect all property-specific information: access, utilities, insurance, HOA rules, owner preferences, existing bookings to honor.

• Set and manage owner expectations on timeline, pricing strategy, and what they can expect from Divergent through onboarding and beyond.

• Serve as the primary contact for the owner during the onboarding period, then warm-handoff to ongoing management.

Listing Build & Optimization

• Build new listings on Airbnb, VRBO, and direct booking platforms (owner retains listing ownership in the co-hosting model).

• Coordinate professional photography when needed; optimize photo order for conversion.

• Write compelling listing titles, descriptions, house rules, and amenity tagging.

• Verify all listing details against the actual property — amenities present, safety items in place, access instructions accurate.



Pricing Setup

• Configure dynamic pricing tools (PriceLabs, Wheelhouse, or equivalent) with correct base rates, minimum stays, and seasonality curves.

• Identify local events and demand patterns; configure event-based pricing rules.

• Partner with the pricing lead on mid-term rental (MTR) strategy when applicable.

• Set and document pricing assumptions so ongoing management can adjust as data comes in.

Operational Handoff

• Document everything the cleaning, maintenance, and guest experience teams need: supply PAR levels, cleaner assignment, maintenance vendors, guest communication templates, property-specific quirks.

• Ensure the first cleaning, first inspection, and first supply order are complete before the first guest arrives.

• Walk the ongoing management team through the property in a formal handoff meeting within the first 14 days.

Process Improvement

• Maintain and improve the onboarding SOP — what worked, what broke, what changed.

• Build reusable templates, checklists, and automations that reduce onboarding time without reducing quality.

• Report monthly on onboarding pipeline, time-in-process, and any bottlenecks.



Required Experience & Skills

• 2+ years of short-term rental operations experience — hands-on, not adjacent.

• Demonstrated experience building or launching Airbnb, VRBO, or 
 listings from scratch.

• Working knowledge of a PMS (Hostaway, Guesty, Hospitable, or OwnerRez).

• Hands-on experience with a dynamic pricing tool (PriceLabs, Wheelhouse, Beyond).

• Strong written English and excellent spoken English — you will be on owner-facing calls.

• Project management discipline — you can manage 3–5 simultaneous onboardings without dropping details.

• Comfort with checklists, SOPs, and repeatable processes.



Preferred (Not Required)

• Prior experience in hospitality, property management, or client onboarding.

• Experience with Breezeway, Slack, Salesmsg, and GetClearing.

• Design eye for listing photos and descriptions.

• SEO or listing optimization experience on major OTA platforms.



Non-Negotiable Traits

• Organizational discipline. You manage multiple onboardings without letting any slip.

• Owner-facing confidence. You can hold a call with a $500K+ property owner and make them feel informed, respected, and confident in Divergent.

• Process thinker. You build systems, not one-offs.

• Ownership mindset. When onboarding breaks, your reflex is "how do I fix this and prevent it," not "who told me to do this."

• Professionalism under owner pressure. Owners can be anxious during onboarding. You stay steady, clear, and solution-oriented.



What We Offer

• Competitive hourly rate for a qualified STR operator.

• One of the most strategically important roles in the company — onboarding directly drives growth and retention.

• Quarterly performance reviews with bonus opportunities.

• Direct access to the owner (Nick) for strategic decisions.

• Long-term role with growth potential as we scale to 200+ properties.

• Professional development, coaching, and 1:1 time with leadership.



Hiring Process

1. Resume and written application — confirm STR onboarding experience.

2. 2-minute Loom video introduction — walk us through a property you onboarded from scratch.

3. First interview (30 minutes) with Divergent leadership.

4. Paid trial project ($75–100): Draft a 5-step onboarding plan with timelines for a sample property, write the owner welcome
, and identify the top 3 risks for this specific property.

5. Reference checks — minimum 2 written references with specific questions.

6. Owner call with Nick — cultural fit and final decision.

7. 30-day paid trial starts.

title: Cleaning Operations Lead
description:
We are a short-term rental co-hosting company managing roughly 56 properties across 15 states. We operate under a co-hosting model — property owners retain their listings, reviews, and branding while we handle operations, guest experience, pricing, cleaning, maintenance, and reporting. We compete on quality, not quantity, and we are growing toward a pod-based structure of 50 properties per operational team.



We are hiring for a Cleaning Operations Lead who will own one of the highest-impact functions in the company. Cleaning quality is the single largest driver of guest reviews and owner trust — and this role owns it end-to-end.



Role Mission

Deliver consistent, hotel-grade cleaning quality across every property, every turnover, while keeping a reliable cleaner network, disciplined supply operations, and proactive inspection coverage. You are the owner of cleaning as a product.

Key Outcomes (What Success Looks Like in 90 Days)



You will be measured on these outcomes — not task completion:

• Inspection pass rate above 95?ross all managed properties, month-over-month.

• Cleaner retention above 90% — cleaners stay because working with us is professional and well-coordinated.

• Zero guest-impacting supply stockouts — PAR levels hold across the portfolio.

• Guest cleaning complaint rate under 2% of stays in any rolling 30-day window.

• Sub-60-minute response time on cleaning-related escalations during business hours.

• Supply order accuracy above 98% — correct items, correct property, correct sizes, correct bedding, on first attempt.

• Emergency (1-hour/3-hour) delivery rate under 5% of total supply orders.



Core Responsibilities

Cleaner Network Management

• Source, vet, and onboard new cleaners in markets where we operate.

• Maintain strong, professional relationships with existing cleaners — they choose to work with us because the coordination is clean.

• Monitor cleaner performance using inspection results, guest feedback, and turnover quality metrics.

• Replace underperforming cleaners proactively, not reactively.

Quality Control & Inspections

• Own the property inspection program per SOP 2.7 — ensure monthly inspections happen on schedule with complete photo documentation.

• Define and enforce cleaning quality standards in measurable terms.

• Identify patterns in quality issues and drive root-cause fixes, not repeat fixes.

• Partner with Guest Experience on any guest-reported cleaning issues and close the feedback loop to the cleaner.

Supply Operations

• Maintain PAR levels for all properties per SOP 2.5 — the right supplies at the right time, at the right cost.

• Place supply orders through Sam's Club, Walmart, and Amazon following ordering policy and delivery rules.

• Verify every order against property-specific requirements (bedding sizes, color schemes, brand preferences) before purchase — no mismatched items.

• Reconcile orders in GetClearing with correct memos and property assignments within 2 business days of posting.

• Run monthly supply audits against property inventory in Breezeway.

Coordination & Communication

• Coordinate cleaner schedules against check-ins, check-outs, inspections, and maintenance windows.

• Daily updates in Slack on cleaning status, issues, and resolutions.

• Respond to operational escalations within 60 minutes during business hours.

• Participate in weekly leadership check-ins and monthly scorecard reviews.

Required Experience & Skills

• 2+ years of short-term rental operations experience — hands-on, not adjacent.

• Direct experience with Breezeway (cleaning checklists, task templates, inspection workflows).

• Working knowledge of Hostaway or a similar PMS.

• Fluency in Slack, Salesmsg, and GetClearing (or willingness to become fluent within 14 days).

• Experience managing 3–10 cleaners simultaneously across multiple properties or markets.

• Strong written English — clear, concise, professional communication with cleaners, tea
mbers, and (occasionally) owners.

• Demonstrated attention to detail — you catch mismatched bedding, wrong sizes, missing linens before they become problems.

• Supply chain / purchasing experience — comfort with bulk ordering, vendor management, receipt reconciliation.

Preferred (Not Required)

• Prior experience as a cleaner or cleaning business operator.

• Inspection or QA background in hospitality or property management.

• Experience building or maintaining SOPs in Notion, Google Docs, or similar.

• Spanish language skills (some cleaner networks are bilingual).

Non-Negotiable Traits

• Attention to detail. This role cannot be done by someone who misses things. You will be judged partly on how few errors slip past you.

• Ownership mindset. When something is wrong, your reflex is "how do I fix this and prevent it," not "who told me to do this."

• Process discipline. You follow SOPs, document exceptions, and build better SOPs when gaps appear.

• Vendor-friendly communication. Cleaners choose to stay or leave based on how they are treated. You are respectful, clear, and firm.

• Calm under pressure. Turnovers go wrong. Guests arrive early. Cleaners cancel. You handle it without drama.



What We Offer

• Competitive hourly rate for a qualified STR operator.

• Clear expectations, measurable outcomes, and supportive leadership.

• A role with real ownership — this is the cleaning function, not a support position.

• Quarterly performance reviews with bonus opportunities.

• A long-term home for someone who wants to grow with a company targeting 200+ properties by 2028.

• Professional development, coaching, and 1:1 time with leadership.

title: Amazon Advertising Specialist (PPC Manager)
description:
About the Role

We're looking for a sharp, results-driven Amazon Advertising Specialist who doesn't just manage campaigns — they own them. You'll be responsible for the full advertising lifecycle of our luxury Amazon brands, from strategy and execution to weekly reporting and hitting aggressive growth targets. If you understand how to market premium products without cheapening the brand, and you love turning ad spend into revenue, this role is for you.

What You'll Be Doing

You'll take complete ownership of our Amazon PPC campaigns across Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, and Sponsored Display. This means building campaigns from scratch, optimizing existing ones, and making data-backed decisions daily. You'll manage campaigns inside Pacvue, leveraging its automation, rules, and reporting features to run efficient, scalable advertising. Every week, you'll deliver clear, actionable reports that show what's working, what isn't, and what you're doing about it. You'll own our brand presence on Amazon -  including competitor research and monitoring, keyword strategy, and listing alignment - and you'll be directly accountable for hitting our ACOS, TACOS, and revenue targets. When targets are missed, we expect you to know why and have a plan.

You're a Great Fit If You

Have at least 2 years of hands-on Amazon PPC experience managing real ad budgets, with specific experience in the luxury or premium goods space. You understand that luxury brands require a different advertising posture — protecting brand perception, targeting the right customer, and avoiding discount-driven tactics that erode brand equity. You have direct, working experience with Pacvue and can hit the ground running without a learning curve. You're comfortable inside Amazon Seller Central and the advertising console, and you understand keyword research, bid strategy, search term harvesting, and negative keyword management deeply. You can build and maintain weekly performance reports in Excel or Google Sheets that are clean and easy to understand. You communicate proactively -  if something is going wrong, you raise it early, not after the fact.

As our business expands, your responsibilities and compensation will grow along with it. title: Marketing Expert / Content + SEO + Growth Specialist (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
About Us

We are Lab 916, an Amazon growth and ecommerce agency working with fast-scaling brands. We move fast, expect ownership, and focus on results.



We are hiring a Content + SEO + Growth Specialist to own our inbound engine end-to-end.



What You’ll Own



1. Content + SEO



Weekly keyword research using Ahrefs (Content Gap + Keywords Explorer)

Edit ~30 blog drafts/month (about 7 every Sunday) so they sound human, not AI

Publish content to Framer CMS with proper meta titles + descriptions

Submit URLs via IndexNow + Google Search Console

Maintain technical SEO: broken links, redirects, orphan pages, sitemap fixes

Optimize CTR via title/meta rewrites

Run monthly SEO health checks



Goal:

Grow DR from 18 to 35 and organic traffic from ~100 to 5,000/month by end of year



2. Link Building + Digital PR



Build 20 to 25 new referring domains/month

Run HARO / Connectively / Qwoted outreach (target 5 to 10 links/month)

Submit Lab 916 to agency directories (Clutch, G2, DesignRush, etc.)

Pitch guest posts (Jungle Scout, Helium 10, Seller Labs, eComEngine)

Pitch podcasts (AM/PM, Serious Sellers, Ecommerce Fuel, etc.)



3. LinkedIn + Organic Social



Ghostwrite 3 LinkedIn posts/week in founder voice

Schedule posts, respond to comments, engage with ICP accounts

Repurpose high-performing posts into blog content



4.
 Marketing + Nurture



Monthly client newsletter

Prospect nurture sequences (post-Instantly replies)

Trend-based
 blasts (Amazon updates, fee changes, etc.)

Segment lists (clients, lost leads, warm replies, subscribers)



5. Competitive Intelligence



Weekly intel digest on competitors (Envision Horizons, Pattern, etc.)

Monitor reviews on Clutch, G2, Trustpilot

Track Amazon ecosystem updates

Identify positioning opportunities and content angles



6. Brand Assets + Case Studies



Collect testimonials, logos, and client assets

Produce 1 long-form case study/month with real data

Maintain asset library (badges, team photos, product shots)



Must-Have Skills



Native or near-native English (non-negotiable)

2+ years in digital marketing (B2B or ecommerce preferred)

Hands-on experience with Ahrefs + Google Search Console

Strong content editor who can humanize AI drafts

Solid ghostwriter who can match tone and voice

Organized and comfortable in ClickUp

Must overlap at least 4 hours with PST



Education Requirement



Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a closely related field from a reputable university

or

Master’s degree in Marketing (preferred)



Nice-to-Have



Amazon ecosystem knowledge (FBA, PPC, Seller Central)

Framer CMS or Webflow experience

HARO / digital PR experience

Basic Canva skills



How to Apply



Share 2 to 3 writing samples (blog and LinkedIn preferred)

Briefly explain how you would grow a site from 100 to 5,000 monthly traffic

Include the word “916 Growth” in your application

title: Ecommerce Executive Administrative Manager Assistant
description:

This role is not for someone average — we are looking for someone who is sharp, fast, and willing to work hard and improve every day.



What We’re Looking For:

Strong attention to detail (this is critical)

Ability to think independently and solve problems quickly

Comfortable receiving and applying feedback and criticism

Strong critical thinking skills

Good with numbers and data accuracy

Fluent in written and spoken English

Reliable, loyal, and looking for a long-term role

Fast learner with a strong work ethic

Good with miscellaneous tasks and organization



Experience is flexible — attitude and ability matter more.



What a Typical Day Looks Like



Log in and check in with me directly

Spend time on product purchasing and sourcing tasks

Handle data entry, logging, and uploads

Complete administrative and miscellaneous business tasks

Wrap up with any remaining tasks for the day



Responsibilities



Product purchasing and research

Data entry and data management

Admin tasks and organization

Miscellaneous business support tasks



Requirements

Stable internet (good WiFi)

Compensation & Benefits

Monthly performance bonuses

Raises every 6 months

Paid Time Off (PTO)

Paid holidays

Flexible work hours (more flexibility earned over time)

Low-stress, supportive work environment

How to Apply

Why are you a good fit for this role?

This role is for someone young (prefer someone that is 25 years old and younger), hungry and motivated, very smart, and super excited and interested to do their best every day. Also bonus points if you are a fun person and a good person to work with!

title: administrative assistant
description:
Virtual Assistant (Retreat & Event Operations)

About the Role

I am  looking for a high-level, proactive Virtual Assistant to serve as the Operational Lead for an upcoming exclusive wellness retreat for women in medicine. You will be ensuring that the backend runs seamlessly so that our attendees receive a  excellent transformative experience from the 
nt they register.



Primary Responsibilities

Landing Page & Registration: Build and manage a professional landing page  that integrates with our payment gateway (I have created the gateway already)



Attendee Management: Track registrations, manage the attendee roster, and handle all "back-end" communication—including confirmation
s, packing lists, and pre-retreat surveys.



Hotel Liaison: Coordinate with the resort to ensure our "KT26" group block is being utilized correctly and assist attendees with basic booking inquiries. also that our requirements and expectations from the hotel are fulfilled



Customer Support: Serve as the primary point of contact for
 and text inquiries from busy attendees, providing "sisterly" yet professional support.



Digital Organization: Manage the retreat’s digital folders, schedule Zoom touchpoints, and ensure all "The Protocol" documents are polished and ready for distribution.



The Ideal Candidate

You have previously supported  event planners. You understand that our "clients" are busy, high-achieving women who value precision and warmth and patience



Tech-Fluent: You are comfortable with social media, simple web design, and CRM/Email marketing tools.



Proactive Problem Solver: You don't just find problems; you bring solutions. You can anticipate what a "tired doctor" needs before she asks.



Direct & Professional Communicator: You can mirror my brand’s voice—sisterly, excellence, caring, and grounded.



Project Timeline & Terms

Duration: Immediate start through July 2026 (Post-retreat wrap-up).



Hours: [5-10 hours/week, increasing as the retreat approaches].



How to Apply

Please send a brief cover letter and video detailing:



Your experience managing registrations for live events.



The tools you recommend for a quick, high-end landing page setup.



Why you are the best fit to support me on this journey

title: Local SEO Specialist (Website SEO + Google Business Profile)
description:
We are a results-driven digital marketing agency focused on generating leads for local service businesses ranging from home builders, horse training, solar, auto repair, ecommerce, to legal and investment services.



We are looking for a Senior Local SEO Manager who can take ownership of rankings, visibility, and performance across both:



Google Business Profiles (Google Maps / Map Pack)

Local business websites (organic search)



This is a high-level role.

You will be responsible for outcomes—not tasks.



Your Role:



You will own and improve the performance of 25–40 local SEO campaigns, including:



Google Business Profile (GBP) / Maps SEO

Diagnose and improve map pack rankings

Optimize categories, services, and geo relevance

Build and execute review acquisition strategies

Identify ranking bottlenecks and fix them



Website / Local SEO

Optimize service + location pages for rankings and conversions

Improve internal linking and topical relevance

Identify technical and on-page SEO opportunities

Align website SEO with GBP performance



Strategy & Performance Ownership

Identify what is holding rankings back—and solve it

Prioritize high-impact actions (not busywork)

Continuously improve performance across accounts

Recommend and implement process improvements



What Success Looks Like

Clients consistently rank in the top 3 of Google Maps (map pack)

Website rankings improve for high-intent local keywords

Measurable increases in calls, leads, and visibility

Clear, consistent performance improvements across accounts



Requirements



Must Have (Non-Negotiable)

Proven success ranking businesses in Google Maps / GBP (map pack)

Strong experience with local website SEO (on-page + structure)

Experience managing 20+ SEO clients simultaneously

Ability to diagnose ranking issues and prioritize solutions

Fluent English (you must be able to clearly explain strategy and decisions)



Preferred Experience

Background working in a marketing agency

Experience in home services or local service niches

Familiarity with tools like SEMrush, Ahrefs, or BrightLocal



Important – Application Requirements



To be considered, you MUST include:



1. Proven Results

Provide at least 2–3 examples of:

Google Business Profiles you ranked

Keywords + city

Before and after positions

2. Strategy Explanation (Video Required)

Submit a short Loom/video answering:

“How do you take a local business from outside the top 3 into the map pack?”

3. Real-World Thinking

Answer this:

“If a client is not improving after 60 days, what steps do you take?”



Applications without proof of results will NOT be considered.



Why Join Us

You will have real ownership over results—not micromanagement

Work with a team focused on performance, not busywork

Opportunity to grow into a leadership role

Long-term, stable position with room to scale



Final Note

We are not looking for someone to “do SEO tasks.”

We are looking for someone who understands how to drive rankings, traffic, and leads—and can execute at a high level.



We will be taking up to 100 applicants in the next 7-14 days and then closing out this opportunity.



If you have a proven track record in Local SEO and Google Maps optimization, we want to speak with you.

title: Cold Caller Lead Gen B2B

We are hiring a full-cycle sales representative who can:

title: Part-Time Leasing Specialist
description:
Part-Time Lead to Lease Specialist

Job Type: Part-Time

Saturday and Sunday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, with a 1-hour break (time zone to be finalized)



Job Overview: 

We are seeking a confident and driven Lead to Lease Specialist to manage inbound inquiries and proactively engage prospective residents through outbound calls. In this role, you will play a key part in converting leads into scheduled property tours by delivering a seamless and informative experience. 



The ideal candidate is highly comfortable on the phone, possesses strong communication skills, and can clearly explain the self-guided showing process while building rapport and a sense of urgency with prospects. This position requires a proactive approach, attention to detail, and the ability to effectively nurture leads throughout the leasing journey to support occupancy goals.



This role is ideal for a driven leasing professional who thrives in a fast-paced, performance-based environment and is motivated by turning leads into qualified applicants.



Key Responsibilities:

, and online leads)

Respond promptly to new leads and follow up consistently to drive engagement

Clearly explain the self-showing process and assist prospects with scheduling tours

Maintain accurate lead tracking and notes within company systems

Collaborate with Property Managers and Marketing to ensure alignment on availability and listing details

Follow up with prospects after tours to encourage applications



Performance Expectations:

Success in this role is measured by conversion metrics, specifically:

Leads converted to scheduled tours

Tours converted to completed applications



Required Skills & Qualifications:

Prior property management or leasing experience required

Experience with AppFolio (required)

Familiarity with Rently and Tenant Turner (preferred)

Comfortable handling both inbound and outbound calls

Strong phone presence with the ability to build rapport quickly

Clear, professional communication skills

Highly organized with strong follow-up discipline



Nice to Have:

Experience in a lead-to-lease or leasing conversion-focused role

title: US CPA Tax Prep + Bookkeeping | Drake Tax + TaxDome
description:
We are a US-based fractional CFO and CPA accounting firm serving small and mid-size businesses. We work with a range of clients including home service companies, healthcare businesses, and professional services firms. We're growing and looking for a sharp, detail-oriented CPA based in the Philippines to join our team on a long-term, direct-hire basis. We will start part-time and transition the role into full-time within a 60 day basis



THE ROLE

You'll serve as a remote tax preparer and bookkeeper handling a portfolio of US individual and business clients. You'll work directly with our firm leadership and have real ownership over your client files.



Responsibilities include:

• Preparing US individual returns (Form 1040, Schedules A/B/C/D/E/SE)

• Preparing business returns (Form 1065 partnerships, 1120-S S-corps, 1120 C-corps)

• Monthly/quarterly bookkeeping in QuickBooks Online

• Bank reconciliations, categorization, and financial statement prep

• Managing client document collection and communication in TaxDome

• Entering and reviewing tax data in Drake Tax software

• Tracking jobs through TaxDome pipelines and organizers

• Flagging issues, missing docs, or unusual items to the reviewing CPA



? REQUIREMENTS

• Active Philippines CPA license (PRC) — required

• US CPA or Enrolled Agent designation — strong plus, not required

• Hands-on experience with Drake Tax software — required

• Hands-on experience with TaxDome — required

• QuickBooks Online proficiency — required

• Minimum 2 years preparing US federal tax returns

• Strong written English communication

• Reliable internet connection and a quiet, dedicated workspace

• Ability to work overlapping hours with US Eastern or Central time zone



???? COMPENSATION

• $1,000–$1,800/month USD depending on experience and credentials

• Direct hire — paid in USD, no agency fees deducted

• Paid US holidays + PTO

• Performance bonuses during tax season

• Long-term engagement with room to grow into a senior preparer or reviewer role



???? HOW TO APPLY

Send the following to 

1. Your updated resume or CV

2. A brief note (3–5 sentences) describing your experience with Drake Tax and TaxDome

3. A list of the return types you've prepared (1040, 1065, 1120-S, etc.) and approximate volume per tax season

4. Your expected monthly rate in USD - 15 hours a week to start

5. Your availability start date



Applications without Drake Tax and TaxDome experience will not be reviewed. We respond to every qualified applicant within 3 business days.



We are not working with recruiters or staffing agencies for this role.

title: Setup Specialist (Fresh Graduates or with Experienced, Permanent WFH)
description:
JOB OVERVIEW

Setup Specialist (Initial Disclosure Specialist)



Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Accounting, or a related field ONLY. Applicants without this degree requirement will not be considered.

We are looking for a highly detail-oriented Setup Specialist to join our mortgage operations team. Your main responsibility is to prepare and review initial mortgage disclosures to ensure accuracy, compliance, and timely delivery to borrowers. This is a great opportunity for someone who wants long-term stability, growth, and a work-from-home setup.



Job Responsibilities



Prepare and review initial mortgage disclosures with strict attention to accuracy and compliance.

Ensure disclosures are delivered on time and in accordance with federal and state regulations.

Coordinate with loan officers, processors, and internal teams to collect required information.

Stay updated on mortgage regulations and compliance requirements.

Identify, investigate, and resolve any discrepancies in disclosure documents.

Help improve internal policies, checklists, and workflows for the disclosure process.

Maintain well-organized and accurate documentation of disclosure activities.



Qualifications



Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Accounting, or related field (required).

Excellent attention to detail with strong organizational skills.

Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Ability to work independently and collaborate in a fast-paced environment.

Proficient in Microsoft Office and comfortable learning new software.

Experience in US mortgage industry is a plus but not required.



Benefits



Competitive salary + performance incentives

Paid time off and holidays (after regularization)

Work-from-home setup

Long-term position with opportunities for promotion

Paid training and periodic salary reviews



Work Schedule



Full-time (40 hours/week)

Night shift / Rotational shift

Monday to Friday

At least 16GB RAM

Intel Core i5 4th Generation or newer (or equivalent AMD processor)

Reliable computer capable of handling mortgage systems and multiple documents

 sent directly through OnlineJobs.ph.

title: Content Clipper (Short-Form Video) — 60,000 PHP/Month
description:
BENEFITS



- Full-time position: 7.5 hours/day

- Schedule: Monday to Friday, 8 AM – 5 PM (Philippine Time)

- 60,000 PHP/month after the first month + performance-based bonuses

- 40,000 PHP during the first (test) month

- Paid every two weeks via Wise

- Fully remote position

- Young, supportive team

- Clear growth path with opportunities to expand your role



ROLE OVERVIEW



You will extract short, high-value clips from a YouTube channel.



Your main responsibility is to:



- Watch long-form YouTube videos

- Deeply understand our target audience (pain points, goals, objections)

- Identify valuable, engaging, and on-brand 
nts

- Select clips that align with our short-form SOPs

- Turn them into 15–45 second Instagram Reels

- Add clean and accurate subtitles

- Ensure smooth audio

- Deliver 2–3 clips per day



You are not creating content from scratch.

You are selecting and packaging the best parts — strategically.



TASKS



- Clip 2–3 short videos per day (15–45 seconds each)

- Follow our short-form SOPs consistently

- Select clips based on audience relevance and brand alignment

- Clearly explain your thought process when submitting clips:

- Ensure subtitles are accurate and easy to understand

- Ensure a smooth audio experience

- Review your work carefully before submitting



Note: There may be additional small tasks as needed.



REQUIREMENTS



- Excellent English skills (C1 or above)

- Strong understanding of audience psychology and content-market fit

- Ability to follow and execute based on SOPs

- Previous experience in short-form editing or video clipping

- Strong judgment for identifying valuable, on-brand content

- Detail-oriented with critical thinking skills

- Ability to work independently while staying communicative

- Efficient and solution-focused mindset



HOW TO APPLY



ROUND 1: Fill in the application form and record a Loom introducing yourself:




/to/v0OA9cFs



Important note: Put “VC” + [Your Name & Family Name] in the “Name” field.



ROUND 2: Paid test task. You will receive long-form videos and create short clips in the same style.



MORE ABOUT US

title: Client Success Manager (Coaching and Feedback) – $1,100/month
description:
Full-Time | Long-Term Role



Start at $935 per month base.

With bonuses, most earn around $1,100 per month.

Top performers grow to $1,800–$2,500 within 6–12 months.



Paid every two weeks via Wise. Always on time.



This is not a short-term job.

We build long-term careers.



You’ll have clear systems, call scripts, training, and daily team support.

You won’t be guessing what to do.



About Us



Young Publisher - 



We are an international digital marketing company.

We work with content creators and online brands.

We have hired Filipino professionals for over 5 years.

We focus on stability, structure, and growth.



What You’ll Gain



- Full-time stable income

- Monday to Friday schedule

- Flexible hours (one daily meeting required)

- No graveyard shifts

- Reliable payments via Wise every two weeks.

- Performance bonuses

- Clear path to increase your income

- All tools provided

- If you perform well, your income grows.



What You’ll Do



- Manage 40–50 clients

- Run onboarding calls (script provided)

- Review client content and give clear feedback

- Reply to messages within 24 hours

- Host weekly group calls and 1-on-1 sessions

- Track client progress and update reports

- Join daily team check-ins

- Your role is to help clients succeed and stay long-term.

- Proactively follow up with clients to keep them on track and help them take action



Important Note: This role requires regular proactive follow-up with clients. If you're not comfortable with this, please consider carefully before applying - it's a core part of the position.



What We’re Looking For



- Strong English (written and spoken)

- Confident on Zoom calls

- Organized and detail-oriented

- Reliable internet

- Quiet workspace

- Consistent and accountable



Bonus if you have experience with short-form content, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, or AI tools like ChatGPT and CapCut.



How to Apply



Send a message with “CSM” as the subject line.



Include ONLY:



#1: Your CV

#2: Years of short-form content experience

#3: Years of client management experience

#4: One sentence explaining why you are a strong fit

#5: Your portfolio



Applications that do not follow this format will not be reviewed.
